Took back for refund and was told Id lost my voucher - one time only use. Thats one loyal customer theyve lost. Ill never shop there again.

You send me an email after seeing the review here and you tell me that you ll figure it out. What is there to figure out? Do you need a college degree to understand this simple matter?

review_title: Worst online shopping experience Ive ever had
review_text: I ordered 5 things on 06/13/21, got email the next day saying it was packaged, with a shipping label, and waiting to get sent out. Then I waited and waited, and eventually filled out their online customer service form on 06/24/21. I immediately got an email saying my order was lost and I would get a refund (that would at most take 7 business days to get it. I never got it, so I emailed yesterday (07/06/21) asking where it was, and magically my order, that was said to be lost and they werent sending any more, was on my doorstep only about 12-14 hours later. It took almost a month to get my items, and I had to complain multiple times. If anything doesnt fit, I wont return them, because I dont trust them to get my money back. Worst online shopping experience ever, Im not sure how a company as big as h&m can pull stuff like this to so many people.

I couldn't put in the order, kept getting an error about the optional phone number you had to put in. When I wanted to try again, my entire shopping cart was empty again. Endless!!!

Be very careful when ordering from H&M online because if something is wrong on your order you will have to contact their customer service team several times, who are absolutely awful and seem to get a kick out of wasting your time! My wife ordered some tops online. When the order confirmation email came through she realised the order was being sent to our old address, which admittedly was her mistake as the address fields were auto-filled to our old address on her mobile. As soon as she noticed though she called H&M customer services to get the delivery address changed or to just get the order cancelled so we could get it delivered to our new address. This is where the ridiculousness began. When they picked up the guy in customer services eventually told my wife that they cannot change the delivery address or cancel the order cos that's not how they work. That was after he kept butting in while she was simply trying to explain the situation. Instead, he said she had to wait until she gets an email say the order is out for delivery and then to contact their customer services again. This guy also said he had made a note on their system so when she calls back it will be easy to sort out. NOT TRUE! This morning my wife got the "out for delivery" email and called them straight away. A disinterested woman, who sounded like a small child answered and told my wife that there were no notes on their system and after nearly 20 minutes of putting my wife on hold told her that the delivery could not be sent to another address or cancelled at the moment as it's out for delivery! What a joke. Instead she told my wife that she would contact the courier somehow to get them to cancel the delivery and not to worry as it will all be sorted and the only thing that will happen now is an email will be sent through to say the order was cancelled and a refund will be issued. NOT TRUE!! Half hour later my wife received an email from the courier saying the order will be delivered today between these times and the only options were to leave the order in a safe place or with a neighbour. No option to cancel or deliver elsewhere. So my wife had to call H&M yet again. A scottish guy answered and was helpful to a point. He basically said the order will still be delivered to that address but a refund will be issued anyway and a confirmation of this will be emailed to her. So he's saying a random person at our old address will be given our order, whom of course they will just accept because that's the world we live in nowadays. And not only will they have our order, but they will also have a receipt with all my wife's details on it. My wife tried to explain this but the guy just played it off as nothing to worry about. My wife had no choice but to go along with this companies policy so she is now waiting for a refund confirmation email. This is the last time we will buy anything from H&M cos we can't trust that our orders will go through smoothly and we'd rather not be fobbed off by their awful customer services team again. Every other online company who we have ordered from have always been able to sort out any errors or issues, even if it meant them calling us back after they have sorted it out...NOT making the customer call THEM back multiple times for no resolution. In summary. Don't buy online from H&M as you could end up with them seriously wasting your time if there's ever a problem. Maybe buy from the actual store instead. UPDATE: My wife just got a confirmation email that the order has been delivered...to strangers at our old address! Crazy. I will update this again if and when our refund confirmation email comes through. Awful company!

review_title: If I could give zero stars I would
review_text: Extremely poor customer service and I lost count how many times I had to contact their customer service team to get a resolution. I was going around in circles and ended up giving up. Basically I sent back some jeans I ordered (the size was too small). I originally purchased them on a Visa Gift card but I misplaced the gift card. I know this was my fault but I proactively contacted customer service before returning the jeans to see what my options were. A consultant told me over the phone that they couldn't refund onto a different credit card but could instead give the refund in the form of a H&M gift card and told me to email the customer service team which I did. I received in writing from that team that they could process it onto a H&M gift card. With this information I proceeded to return the jeans. Long story short I was never given the H&M gift card and chased this up so many times I lost count. I was hung up on, provided "case numbers" that meant nothing and fake promises. One time I emailed and the response was to email a particular email address to raise the issue...would you believe that this particular email address that they gave me was the very same one I had already emailed?! It was incredibly frustrating, I kept getting told it wasn't their policy and to chase up the lost card with my bank, even though I had explained to them that this wasn't possible because it wasn't a true credit card but a visa gift card (this was apparently a very difficult concept for them to understand). I told them I had the refund promised to me in writing and provided this to them but they didnt care. I understand it was my fault for losing the card but had I been given correct info when I initially called before returning the jeans, I wouldnt have returned them....I could've sold them to recoup the costs. Instead I was left with no jeans and no refund. I asked them as a good will gesture (and since I had it in writing and 2 different consultants giving me the wrong information), if they could write off a gift card and send it to me to keep a customer happy. Apparently H&M can't afford $55 to do this! I would not recommend ordering online from them in case you ever need to contact their "customer service" team

After I tried the jeans on I wanted to exchange them for a L34 within the bull ring store I brought them with me though I first looked to see if I could find some in that size and I couldn't. I proposed that they look the product up in store via the product code on the system and that they then have these delivered in store in which when they arrive that I exchange them for my previously brought pair. H&M need to improve their approach to customer service & satisfaction heavily as well as their choice of employees ranging from sales staff to managers.

review_title: I received a an electronic high streetĶ
review_text: I received a an electronic high street vouchers gift card which allowed me to pick a brand to get a gift card for. I chose H&M and placed an order online (paying half with the voucher and half on my debit card) for collection at a local store within 2-4 days. On day 2 I was notified that Hermes had received my order, on day 3 Hermes notified me there was going to be an unexplained delay. On day 4 the tracker showed they were returning it to H&M without attempting to deliver. Obviously if you live on this planet youll know Hermes are possibly the worst company in existence and I couldnt speak to anyone to find out the reason for this. Spoke to H&M who said Ill receive a H&M email gift card for the amount on the voucher I used and the rest on my card. I was advised it would take up to 10 working days to get the refund once they receive it, which could be in 2 days or 2 years because of Hermes. I asked if it is possible to get a discount code or anything seen as I was going to be out of pocket for this time for no fault of my own and I cannot even make purchase else where as the gift card Im waiting for is to use with them. Was told there is nothing they can do about it end I will just get the voucher/debit card refund in due course. A week later I was notified H&M had received the parcel back and they issued my refund. I placed a new order online when I got the voucher and refund. As H&M do not use high street sizes but S M L etc I had no idea what size to order, but they have size checker on the items. The checker suggested I get XL for all my items. Being chubby I accepted this and placed the order for collection at a store (to bypass Hermes). When I opened the box, the clothes were MASSIVE, you could have fit two people in each of the tops and the joggers literally dropped to my ankles they were so big. I took them back to the store where I wanted to just purchase anything to be over and done with this gift card, but was told it would take 24 hours for a new gift card to be issued. Eventually, I decided I would go back to the store and be done with it. Despite being a large store they only stocked 2 out of the 10 items Id bought online, so I simply bought those two and just bought loads of socks to get my vouchers worth, as I couldnt bring myself to risk another online order with them.

review_title: WARNING PICKPOCKET CIRCULATING IN H&M HAMMERSMITH!!!!
review_text: On 12 November 2022, I decided to go to H&M to browse products on display, after I had been to Boots across the road. I was not browsing in the shop for very long. As I went to the self-checkout till to pay for a cardigan I had chosen, I realised that someone had stolen my brand new iPhone which was located in my locked handbag. When I reported the incident to members of staff, they were absolutely incompetent, incredibly dismissive and unsympathetic. It was apparent they had not been trained to deal with such an event and could not care less. I was in shock as I had not yet had the chance to insure my new phone. I sensibly looked around the store and went back to Boots where a lovely security officer showed me the CCTV footage confirming I had placed my phone in my handbag and locked it after I paid for my items at Boots. At that point I had no doubt the theft occurred in H&M. I was made aware by the same Boots security officer of a pickpocket circulating in H&M- he told me this has had happened several times and this was common knowledge and known to H&M (yet H&M did not think of placing a warning notice on all their entrances to warn other innocent customers...). At that point I was very angry and returned to H&M. I interrogated a young member of staff who eventually confirmed and disclosed having knowledge of a thief operating in their store! When I spoke to another member of staff, a rude short lady with long hair, she very poorly attempted to divert the blame onto the whole of Hammersmith. I was told to speak to yet another member of staff (tattooed male with longish dark hair) to complete the incident report. That person was also useless and the report had once sentence on it at most. I was denied access to the CCTV footage following a formal verbal subject access request as I present in the footage which constitutes personal data. My husband reported the matter to the Police on the same day. The Police contacted H&M by telephone several times to request the original CCTV footage however, the officer was ignored. In the end, the officer had to write to H&M to reiterate her request. The Police took my witness statement and confirmed the screenshot of the thief would be circulated. However, a few days later, on 16th December, the Police officer emailed me informing me that the footage provided by H&M was a copy recorded from the CCTV computer on a phone therefore NOT the original. I was further informed that despite having requested SEVERAL TIMES H&M to keep the original footage, they had not, meaning the footage is forever lost and the thief is running free and will carry on stealing. As a direct result of H&Ms actions, the Police officer is now unable to circulate the screenshot of the suspect, as a positive identification would not be admissible at Court. I notified H&M of their continuous failures and disclosed a copy of the email from the Police confirming these failures. H&M responded to me completely disregarding the issues I had raised and advising me to seek legal advice should I wish to do so. ***DO NOT SHOP IN H&M HAMMERSMITH AS THEY ALLOW THEFT ON PREMISES, DO NOT CARE ABOUT YOU AND DO NOT CO-OPERATE WITH THE POLICE.***

The incident: Purchased L sized shirts and 42 sized waistcoat online from H&M. The shirts were fitting, but the waistcoat was only the size of 36. Therefore, it was too small to use. I chose the size because it is written 42 is equal to L size on the size guide. The explanation from the H&M Company: 1. The waistcoat is a Top category product, so its size should be selected from the Top category. 2. According to the companys policy, a product purchased under the promotion cannot be exchanged with a bigger size (not because of the unavailability). Scenario 1: There is no size guide given for the exact product name waistcoat. Moreover, it is not mentioned that the waistcoat comes under the Top category. Therefore, this is primarily an error by the company; the customers are helpless. (The customers are not garment experts to know the categorization exactly. Being a common man, I thought the size guide for the shirt would also do for the waistcoat). review_title: Gentle Goats Cut Up for Mohair andĶ
review_text: Gentle Goats Cut Up for Mohair and Cashmere Tell H&M to Stop Supporting This Abuse H&M is betraying animals and its customers by reversing its bans on mohair and cashmere, so shoppers should assume that all its mohair and cashmere products came from mutilated animals. The mohair and cashmere industries kill goats urge H&M to stand by its promise and reinstate the bans! Read More + Ms Helena Helmersson H&M Please Stop Supporting Goat Exploitation {contact_data~salutation} {contact_data~title} {contact_data~lastName}, I was extremely disappointed to hear that H&M has gone back on its word and is selling mohair and cashmere again. Your company said that no animal should ever suffer in the name of fashion, but by selling mohair and cashmere, thats exactly what youre enabling. Shearing is extremely stressful to goats, prey animals who are terrified of being pinned down, vulnerable, and defenceless. PETA Asias investigation into the South African mohair industry revealed that workers roughly handled, threw around, mutilated, and cut the throats of fully conscious goats for mohair, and its investigation into cashmere production in China and Mongolia revealed that goats screamed in pain and fear as workers tore their hair out with sharp metal combs. After goats are exploited in these industries, their throats are slit in abattoirs. Standards in the industries may seem well intentioned, but theyre ultimately meaningless. They create a veil of false welfare assurances, doing little or nothing to protect the animals, who continue to be exploited and killed for profit. The Responsible Mohair Standard you tout, like other purportedly responsible standards, fails to protect goats from abuse. Under it, workers can castrate goats and cut off parts of their ears without any pain relief, and it offers no protections for goats during slaughter when workers shoot them in the head or slit their throats. And the similar Good Cashmere Standard that your company helped develop allows untrained farmers to kill baby goats using blunt force trauma and doesnt require the certification of off-farm slaughter sites, which could be similar to facilities where PETA Asia eyewitnesses found workers hitting goats on the head with a hammer and slitting their throats. Please ditch mohair and cashmere, as countless other companies have done, and focus instead on the wonderful vegan materials you already sell. Until you do, Ill urge my friends and family to shop elsewhere. Thank you. review_title: Ordered by express delivery, but no, it didn't take 2-3 days.
review_text: I wanted to find Christmas gifts, was tipped about Zarah. Found clothes and placed an order morning the 16th of Desember, and chose Express DHL delivery as to be sure that the shipment would arrive within Christmas. Since I live further North of my country, shipment takes two additional days when arriving to the capital. Then the package should be at the destination at the 19th, and latest the 20th._x000D_ _x000D_ Well, it didn't turn out that way._x000D_ _x000D_ I blame it half on Zara.com._x000D_ _x000D_ Zara waited till the evening the day after I placed my order before they shipped out the clothes. I don't find it acceptable, as when ordering before noon, I expect an order to be expedited and put out for shipping the same working day. And especially when it's so close to Christmas._x000D_ _x000D_ The other half to blame equally is DHL, as during the travel, the shipment stranded at two different places for one extra day on each place. Meaning two days delay. (I have used DHL many times before, and I have never seen such delay before on those exact locations.) Even standard cheap DHL shipments have turned up faster than this._x000D_ _x000D_ So now I will have to wait till the 27th to bring these gifts to five different people._x000D_ _x000D_ Since I consider Zara to be half the blame, I don't give the lowest rating. But two stars from me is generous of me, considering how I feel about this experience.

Placed an order when the Black Friday discount was on and tracked my order a few days later and the tracking said it had been delivered and that the parcel had been signed for by the customer. Called Zara cust.serv as I hadn't signed for anything, and requested that they sent me a copy of the signature so I could see if I recognised it. They emailed me the signature and I called back the same day to tell them I did not recognise it, and they told me an investigation would be launched and I would hear in 1-2 days. This was on Friday 2nd. I called back on Monday and the person looked into my order and stated no investigation had been launched, apologised for the inconvenience, and assured me the investigation would be launched today. Later that day however I received another email from Zara with the copy of the signature again and to call back if I didn't recognise it. At this point I was confused so called them again the following day and the person apologised and stated they don't know why that email was sent again, but that an investigation had been launched the day before. Stated that he would call me with a solution later that day or on Wednesday. By Thursday morning I called again after not having heard anything. The lady said she'd checked my notes, spoken to a colleague, and that she would refund me. I asked for a confirmation email and she said I would receive one when the refund was processed. A week later, Thursday 15th by now, no refund and no confirmation email. Called Zara again and they said there was no record of my call on Thursday 15th on my notes!!! They told me the case had been closed as I supposedly did not contact them, and when I stated that I had evidence of the phone call being made, the person said a manager would call me later that day or the next day. Friday evening, having not received a phone call, I called again and was basically told that it'd been sent to the relevant department blah blah blah - basically still no refund, and still no communication with a manager. Giving them the weekend and I'll call again Monday afternoon. If there is still no solution, I'm contacting my bank and disputing the transaction. I'd advise you NOT to order from Zara online and whatever you do, keep evidence of ALL correspondence if you do!

Now as I said this was a present from a friend and I told them I wanted the money but they refused. I then told them I wanted my clothes back then and again they refused! I told them that I couldn't just get the money from my friend but the store manager didn't care and told me it wasn't her problem. I said that I thought it would have been the most right thing if they had informed me of all these special rules (that you never see anywhere else in Denmark) she pointed at my reciet but it just said I always could return my online products at a store! Then she pointed at the return reciet I just recieved and yes it said That the money would go back to my friends account but as I pointed out to the store manager I recieved that information after I'd return the clothes!!!!! She was extremely rude and when I insisted to at least give me my clothes back she told me to leave the store or else she would call security. I'm chocked by the horrible return policy and the very rude manager!

review_title: Sent me the wrong order and horrible policy
review_text: Zara had sent me the completely wrong order that I placed on Dec 19th. I got my package on Jan 10. Not only was the package late on delivery, it came with the completely wrong order. Being in ecommerce retail myself, I was very understanding that boxing week can be extremely hectic for warehouses so I wasn't upset initially. I emailed customer service and it took them 3 days to reply. Then I decided to call customer service to get a refund because I simply didn't want to deal with this anymore. Then I was told that I had to return the products first before I get my refund, meaning that I'll have to print out the labels myself (because Zara no longer sends out return labels along with the package), get to a post office, get the tracking number and then call Zara again to let them know the tracking number. Once they have the tracking number, they'll process the return. Simply put, they're putting my refund in hostage. Upon hearing this I was very, very disappointed with how this is being handled. It was a mistake Zara made and they're making the customer do the work to get a refund? How is the reasonable? This is very poor business ethics. Zara is a big business and probably can afford to lose customers over this but over time poor business ethics will not benefit a company in any way. I've been a loyal Zara customer but will never, ever buy from Zara again.

I have been a loyal customer to Zara for many years now, and until recently, I have always been satisfied with the service that I have received and the products that I have purchased, however, I will be terminating my long term relationship as a loyal customer to Zara after the recent experience I have just gone through. I was recently in the U.S. on a family vacation, and purchased a pair of Pants from Zara. The weather in Arizona in August is often over 30 degrees celcius, so I had no need to wear them, however as I had bought pants from Zara on many occasions, I wasnt too concerned about wearing them until I got back home to Vancouver, Canada. When I returned home, I went to put on my new pants, and they fully ripped at the seam. They werent the wrong size, I had simply gone to put them on and they fully ripped. Obviously I was shocked, and somewhat disappointed, as I had spent US $55 (approximately CAD$67) on these pants, and have come to expect a certain level of quality from Zara. However, as I had not worn them, and they were well within the valid return date, I thought I could simply return them and get refunded. What I expected to be a simple return quickly escalated. I went into the Zara close to my house, where I have spent hundreds, if not thousands, of dollars over the past few years. They informed me that since I had purchased the Pants in the U.S. I couldnt return them in Canada, but I could call customer support to see if there was anything that could be done. Frustrated, but somewhat understanding, I called customer support. I was kept on the phone for a very long time, being transferred from person to person, and eventually I spoke to a woman who told me they would take my case into account, gave me a number to confirm my case, and told me I would have to wait up to 3 days to hear back on my request. 5 days later, I finally received an email with a confirmation of my case, I call Zara, once again wait on the phone for a fair bit, and am told that there is nothing that they can do, and if I want to return the pants, I would have to do so in the U.S. Obviously, telling me to do so is a ridiculous solution. Me driving down/ taking a bus to the U.S (the closest store being probably in Seattle) would take up time, as well as cost well over the amount that the jeans were initially worth. I bought a product, the product was poorly made, and instead of accepting the fault and dealing with it professionally and responsibly, Zara managed to avoid dealing with any responsibility that they owed to their customer. I have spoken to friends and found out I am not the only one who has had problems with customer service, as well as the quality of Zara clothing in the past. You would expect a transnational brand like Zara, with worldwide recognition, that fully takes advantage of being able to sell on, and profit off of international markets, to be better equipped to deal with returns in different countries. The brand is in fact Spanish, but nobody is expected to fly to Spain when they need to return an item of clothing. If they can sell clothes throughout the world, they should be better equipped to handle situations like these, which have no doubt happened to many others as well. Zara is a multibillion, transnational corporation. What it would cost them to replace the damaged product I purchased from them would not even make a dent in the profits the company makes. The CEO himself is worth US$ 85 billion, I dont see how the return of a product which I spent US$55 on would be harmful to a man and company who I have come to find out not only relies upon the exploitation of sweatshop workers, but is known to steal designs from Artists, and profit off these designs without paying said Artists. I have been very disappointed with my recent experience, not just due to the lack of quality in the product I have purchased, as well as my personal experience with their poor customer service, but because it has shed light on Zara which has shown me it is nothing more than a greedy company, that doesn't respect their product, employees, or customers. It is a greedy, profit driven company that lacks morals and values. I will be terminating my long-standing, once good relationship with Zara and encourage my friends and family, as well as anyone else who reads this, to do the same.

I am stuck with two pairs of Zara jeans I cannot wear. Very poor customer service. Will not buy again in Zara.

I have made numerous calls to the online team regarding an outstanding refund for a pair of boots that were too large that were returned with other items in the same returns box. The other items were refunded but the boots were not. I keep getting told that it will be another 24 hours before the matter is resolved. I have escalated this to a supervisor by the name of Jasper who promised to call me back to update me and subsequently didn't. I have been told they are investigating the missing item, that perhaps it has been mislaid by the staff in the warehouse.... what concerns me is the same thing happened to me some months previously. The item was declared as missing but then subsequently found over a week later and I was finally refunded. Is there an issue with the company they use who unbox and refund the items? Why does this keep happening? What also worries me is that if I hadn't noticed this error or kept on at them to investigate it... would I ever be refunded for the returned item?

I bought 2 jumpers, same model, different colours - one of them, after first use, already had a whole in it in the colar as the stiching came undone the other had a whole in the ampit after second use as the stiching also came undone.

review_title: I would give them zero starts if Ķ
review_text: I would give them zero starts if possible. The worst experience ever. Order a top 3 weeks ago it never arrived. Mesagged them on facebook they said they will get someone from customer service to check...no answer yet. Tried the online chat I had a brief conversation with 'alesha' and she asked me.for all my details and then she dissapered ...so i was left on the chat alone. What kind of place is this. I undertstand the cust serv is somewhere abroad in india but still they should have had some sort of training or someone must check what they are doing! Or aren't doing! Never again Zara online!!

Items purchased online were fine. I needed to return using their courier collection. easy enough to book on...but...the courier didnt arrive (despite receiving confirmation from Zara that they would collect the following day). I went onto an online chat with someone ('Alesha') who advised that they would rearrange the collection. she also advised that she could not issue a transcript of the chat. Helpful. Anyway, I wouldnt be so niggled if this was the first time this had hapenned. EVERYTIME I have tried to organise a collection, the courier has failed to do so and I have had to rearrange. not easy when you work full time.I actually blame Zara rather than the courier.

I made my order on a Saturday and paid for next day delivery as I was going away on the Tuesday I fully expect to get the parcel on the Monday. It got to the Tuesday afternoon and I contacted the customer service via the online chat. Complete waist of time the lady couldn't string a sentence together and got nothing out of her other than I had to wait. On the Wednesday evening I got very annoyed as they took the money from my account so contact them again and got told they couldn't do anything until they had given Hermes 48 hours to sort themselves out. So I couldn't get a refund or my parcel in that time. The person I spoke to the second time was beyond rude. This is the second issue I've had with Zara (the first being they started randomly taking money from my account when I hadn't ordered anything) and now this rest assured this is me now done with Zara
problems with returning clothes

review_title: I do agree with the most of the reviews Ķ
review_text: I do agree with the most of the reviews on the website. You will definitely have some problems with delivery and clothe exchange. Although, I really like their clothing.

review_title: Never received the package. What's worse, no refund.
review_text: I would give no star if I could. Never received my package and they don't want to refund because they "claim" there was no error found during the shipping process. I work from home and if the driver rings the bell, I will go out to pick up, but if they simply leave it outside, the package will get lost. Common sense for people live in city! So now, I end up paying for over 100 dollars for nothing. No money back, no package. Shame on you Zara!

I ordered a jacket on ZARA.com and chose next day delivery. Now I ordered way before 7pm so they would of received my order and passed onto the courier in time for next day delivery. Well, disappointed I must say my item didnt arrive until 2 days later and since I bought the wrong size I decided to buy the right size and just return the other to store. Just cant believe that the same situation happened once again. I ordered on Friday and since they used Royal Mail First class instead of a courier that guarantees next day delivery, Im going to have to wait this Monday before I get my item. Long story short, they dont bother to use a more accurate courier for next day deliveries. Customer service is terrible, all I got from them was theres not much we can do at this moment, youll have to wait till 8pm before we can contact Royal Mail I receive my Mail/packages from Royal Mail in mid-morning - afternoons. So to say I will receive my package in the evening is just pure utter nonsense. Dont think I will order from them again. Even though my closest store is a far but away, I think I would prefer to drive down instead of going through this messed up situation again.

review_title: If i could give a review in negative Ķ
review_text: If i could give a review in negative stars, i possibly would consider doing that. I ordered a few dresses from ZARA for my daughters photo shoot at her school. The order was placed a week before the event, with the promise of delivery within two days. Reasonable we thought. The day of the photoshoot came and went.No delivery yet. Lasership aint taking calls. ZARA customer service being equally disappointing. A+ for Zero accountability and pathetic customer service !

review_title: Juvenile management not understanding consumer rights
review_text: I just went to get a credit note not a refund for a blazer and some shoes. Apparently they only refund or exchange. All I wanted was a credit note so my partner can select his own items. They refunded the blazer, however as I didnt have a receipt for the shoes they only offered an exchange. How a credit note isnt the same as a exchange I dont know, as the money still stays with Zara. The red head manager with the accent so terrible she could barely speak English lied about offering credit notes where it clearly states on the back of their receipts they offer them if you dont have a receipt. The second manager kept talking over me and offered no help whatsoever other than repeating the redhead. No credit notes. They ended up giving me a receipt for the shoes to exchange at a later date. The shoes arent fit for purpose. I tried to prove purchase with a bank statement as I know with the consumer rights act this is possible. Totally unhelpful useless management. I wanted to put everything on to a gift card and was going to purchase more. I have a strong reluctance to shop in the Trafford centre store again.
Never buy Zara clothes. I used to work in one of their warehouses...man...rats, dust and mould everywhere. Disgusting.

review_title: I will never go into Zara again due to Ķ
review_text: I will never go into Zara again due to the way the staff at the Derby branch speak to you in there store! A man with a bold head, was very aggressive and rude, so I just put down what I was going to buy and walk out. They closed the dressing room 45mins before the shop shut, so I had to try stuff on in the store. It didn't fit so I put them back on the shelves. He came over said we were closing the store and I had to put it back to where they came from. I didn't know exactly where they came from as I had a few items. It was his job to do this and he made the customer feel like it was their responsibility.
(S)hit customer service agents. Fudged up return policy and grievance redressal

review_title: Bought a jacket from Zara online with Ķ
review_text: Bought a jacket from Zara online with gift vouchers and the remainder with a debit card. review_title: They will take your money and not get your order delivered
review_text: Literally the worst customer service I have ever experienced. Did not receive my order and the agent Saira insisted that it was delivered and signed for at my own address and then disconnect me without resolving the issue.

review_title: Zara has always had a reputation for Ķ
review_text: Zara has always had a reputation for the rudest staff ever in store and I can safely say that their customer service staff is just as rude. I ordered a ¬£99 winter coat online from zara 3 weeks ago and having worn it twice there was a hole in the left hand sleeve. I took it back to Zara in Highcross shopping centre in Leicester and they did not have any in store and ordered me another from zara.com. It arrived the next day and this one was also faulty - there was silver paint on the sleeve. I took it back to the Highcross Store again today and was faced with an absolutely shockingly rude manager and store assistant (they could not give me their names but their reference numbers are 60730 + 53275). I explained my frustration that this was the second time in 4 weeks that a ¬£99 coat was faulty and that is not acceptable. The manager laughed in my face and said there is nothing they can do other than exchanging it. I asked for a refund and whilst it was being processed by the store assistant the manager went to complain about me to another manager in the store right in front of my face! How rude and disgusting. Zara should be ashamed at how they treat their customers. I have also rang zara.com and they have supposedly opened a case for me and a supervisor will call me back in 48 hours - I will be shocked if this even happens and judging by their track records I doubt they ever will.
Customer service its HORRIBLE. Any problem, they feed u with 48 hours to contact u. but they NEVER call u. u need chase them.

I called to inquire about boot sizing. Kimberly was CS agent was rude and dismissive when I asked if there was info on how sizing was running for shoes as I have never purchased from Zara. She basically told me to go measure my foot. I asked her if she had info from others who purchased this item--runs large or small (not unreasonable for customer to ask about). She said no and in no way shape or form did she offer to suggest to help or make me feel as though she wanted me to buy the boots. Really? I was close to purchasing two items from Zara and this call cinched my decision to NEVER buy anything from this company. Terrible customer service. ZARA you all need to take a lesson from Zappos. They actually care about their customers.

Very with disappointed with ZARA. As a very frequent customer who spends large amounts online and instore, they've refused to allow a return 2 days past the month, despite my very good reasons.üò° I doubt they'll miss my custom given their profits but they really do need to allow simple things so as not to p*ss off their loyal customers.

What a scam , ordered online 9 items , returned 8 . They have refunded 5 only . When I enquired 3 items are unsellable due to a smell of perfume !! Its a scam without a doubt They were going to keep my money without returning the goods to me or even tell me why they had only credited 5 items Absolutely shocking , will never use again

She refused to listen to what I was saying and kept interrupting me and I also felt she was talking loudly on purpose, so that others in the store were turning around to stare and drawing unneeded attention when I was just trying to exchange an item. I left the store feeling very embarrassed and belittled by someone (who may or may not have been the manager, as I have no name to go on) At one point she said very loudly "I don't need to stand here and expain anything to you" but if she was the manager, this should be her responsibility if she was in charge of the store. I had called the call centre previously and advised it's the managers discretion upon exchanges but this manager choose to be rude, almost shout at me and embarrass me in front of others. My coat has never been worn, has all the tags etc and is still available for sale in the store. I won't be shopping at Zara again.

I returned a coat back to Birmingham store on the 25th October. They advised it would be 10-14 days for the refund of 90. I left it until the 15th day and called Zara who advised that it will be in my account by the end of the day. I phoned up again on the 16th day (today) chasing my refund and spoke to two people. The first person I spoke to, Harris, apologised but couldnt find the refund. He told me to ask my bank for an IRN number to investigate but when I queried what the IRN number was, he didnt know. After multiple attempts, I finally got to speak to a senior who was the rudest person Ive ever spoke to. Her name was Bilshad and she was interruptive and wouldnt let me finish my sentence. She told me that the refund had been processed, despite Harris telling me it hadnt. She told me that I would need to take it up with my own bank and that its gone through on their end. She told me I would get an email with the confirmation that the refund has been processed but I am still awaiting that email and my 90 refund. Extremely unhappy with the service provided as all I want is my refund.

review_title: Zara do you think this customer service is acceptable? Not happy- disgusting service
review_text: Before I received my order there was a problem with the delivery. 5 times I called, was promised a call back but no-one ever did. Update......glad I didnt hold my breath, no call as promised. Called again today & apparently as a good will gesture they will refund my money as they have no stock to replace my missing item. review_title: Terrible customer service at store and phone center
review_text: Terrible customer service at store and over the phone. If you buy something think twice and check products tri times otherwise if you loose receipt there is no way exchange or return. They have a stupid policy. I bought Tshirt for my baby at home when I checked it was made from polyester. I always buy 100% cotton products. I lost receipt and went back to exchange. Usually every store allows for exchange the same amount or you buy more expensive items you pay difference. But it doesn't work with Zara they say you only exchange with the same item. I explained 100times but their awful staff doesn't understand me. I thought Zara is nice and big company but I was very disappointed that absolutely they are far away from civilization. They don't trust to their products for exchange I will never go back to there. Thanks to Tommy Gap Calvin Klein Lacoste and H&M no more Zara.

This company deserves 0 rating. Visiting the Zara store with my daughter, she chose a puffer jacket which was priced as ¬£49.99, however it was Black Friday the next day and we decided to wait to see if this jacket would be in the sale, and yes it was to our delight. We purchased the jacket at 20% off for ¬£39.99 and my daughter was very pleased with her new jacket. Until the following day looking on the website the same jacket was advertised for ¬£25.99 which is half the original price, there was no indication of this being listed in their special price range of any reference to the original price. This is fraudulent pricing practice. We where not offered the difference for the price which in on their pricing policy terms and conditions. I contacted customer service today and they were both very unhelpful, each informing me that Zara have a right to change their prices from the original price without having to advertise this. One did not know the head office address for me to complain and told me to google this. I hope me writing this review will help other people understand this pricing policy should not be allowed. I WILL not be spending any more money with Zara as much as i do like the products, it is the principle that there is a reason for Trading Standards .

I just wanted to know when the parcel will be delivered. And they are just expecting me to wait for it one week at home. I cant track my parcel, they cant call their courier service by phone. It is the worst service experience.

review_title: Lack of consumer rights for International purchases
review_text: Bought a winter coat on holiday in Portugal for our Granddaughter. After a few wears, two fasteners have broken. Have been told that nothing other than a like for like exchange can be done. We are ok with this, but no stock remains in our vicinity. I think these large international companies should take more responsibility when problems occur and be more flexible. They are happy to take the money in the first place, but not very quick in returning it when things go wrong.

review_title: No refund on a faulty item - rude staff
review_text: I returned a blazer (with the label on) but I had chucked the receipt away as I wanted to keep the item. The reason of returning the item, was due to the lining coming undone, so it was faulty. Clearly I had not worn it as the label was on, it was brand new, just faulty. First of all, I wasn't offered a refund and they told me I was not entitled to get money back for a faulty item as I had no receipt. Which goes against basic statutory rights. Secondly, the manager was called and said I wasn't allowed to exchange this for a gift card. I was told I HAD to find something of the same value to replace the item there and then. IF I couldn't find anything, I would have to keep bringing it back with me to exchange for when I did find something. THEN the manager said I probably shouldn't do that as other managers probably wouldn't offer me an exchange and basically had no other choice. So I swapped it for something NOT faulty, which I didn't like, which I've now given to charity. Thanks for nothing Zara. The till staff wren't helpful and they didn't care at all.

review_title: THANK YOU ZARA FOR YOUR LOVE TO YOUR CUSTOMERS
review_text: Next day delivery freaks me out. Its not the first time when I have problems with my delivery. Zaras customer service is so pointless! No one actually helps you or provides any details. I ordered from this store on Thursday my delivery was scheduled for Friday. When I asked customer service when should I expect my parcel they said we do deliveries from 8am to 8pm. So, that means I should sit at home for freaking 12 hours just waiting for it?! And in the result (guess what?) that didnt deliver it! When I asked Zaras customer service what should I suppose to do with this now (cause I was leaving on Saturday for 1 month) they said we will contact you soon.... WHAT?! So the only thing I wanna say THANK YOU ZARA FOR YOUR LOVE TO YOUR CUSTOMERS

review_title: Zara Customer Service does not even pretend to resolve issues.
review_text: I strongly recommend against buying a Zara gift card, or allowing them to transfer the balance of a return to one of their cards. I tried to use my card for the first time today and it came up as invalid on their "activate card" site. I first tried to resolve this issue through chat. The customer service person told me they couldn't help me and that I needed to call customer service. I called customer service and spoke to Lorenzo who told me this was an in-store issue and that I would need to call the store. I asked to speak to a manager, and he told me he is the manager and that I would need to call Corporate in order to file a higher complaint, but he would not give me the phone number. I called Corporate and spoke to Jessie, who told me I need to call customer service for any complaints. When I told her that Lorenzo said I had to call Corporate, Jessie said that she couldn't imagine why he would have said that as only customer service can handle complaints. Is this honestly how Zara runs its business? I am amazed at the run around I just received and still no sign of resolving my issues anywhere in sight.

review_title: Zara has the worst Customer service Ķ

Then i asked can they charged my card for the same amount and send back the new item.. the answer. No! I have to buy it all over again, but this time with regular price. Not discounted price. She wont help me nor compensate me with the same discount for what they did wrong. It is not a big money, but the principle of good business and customer service hasnt been up in the game here. The customer service of Zara is such nightmare! I never imagine before that i would write such a bad review for my fave store. Zara you definately just lose a good customer. Your big time lost!

review_title: Zara has THE worst customer service I

Someone bought us things wed already bought for our daughter and we feel bad asking for the receipt. Zara staff were so rude kept saying its policy, well its never been policy when Ive tried to do it before. Its all brand new season stuff too that she will have twice. I spent 120 pound and they needed a manager to authorise it. So after spending 20 mins in the que I had to wait another 10. Absolute worst customer service ever. I spend at least 200 a week there Im moving over to next instead as they are so much more understanding of the fact certain things dont fit children and you need to change sizes sometimes, without a receipt as long as the tags are on. Absolutely appalling customer service. I truely cant believe how bad the experience with Zara has been. I ordered a parcel last week on next day delivery. I asked to speak to a manager & she promised a call-back within the day ... surprise surprise this didnt happen. I called again today & was told there was no note made for a manager callback & someone would call me within 48hrs. I cant tell you how disgusted I am by the attitude & customer service. Buyer beware, from this experience it seems you are unlikely to receive your order & the staff dont want to know. I will be making a formal complaint!

I was told yesterday that I would get a response today only to be told today it will be tomorrow. When I try and raise a complaint I get ignored, when i try and speak to a manager they say none are available. Whilst all this is going on they have managed to take payment from me. review_title: Zara really needs to work on their Ķ
review_text: Zara really needs to work on their contact and returns policy. I've tried to reschedule a return twice through their online chat service. The first date we agreed, I waited in all day and nobody came. Then the courier came on the day that I cancelled in advance when nobody was home. The second date we agreed was changed by the customer service operative as soon as the chat was ended to a day I'm not going to be home. I give up for today, communication isn't happening.

I have waited home practically all day today (Saturday 23/12) for this order but nothing! I had to call customer service they told me there is nothing they can do as the order hasn't left the warehouse & once the order is dispatched it wont reach me untill after xmas as Royal Mail dont deliver on a sunday!! Zara is the worst company ever they dont do nothing to keep customers happy, they simply dont care! Why do they offer a next day service when they cant fulfil it??! Absolutely ridiculous!

review_title: the clothes at Zara Atlanta store look like something you buy at Walmart
review_text: When Zara open their store in Atlanta Georgia the quality and the service was good, lately they send clothes you can buy at target or Wal-Mart this is a fact, we should stop shopping at Zara until they fix the issue, The quality is not there anymore.fix it or you will loose a lot of customers

Bought three dresses online and received something different. Complained to customer services, and they didn't accept it was a manufacturers fault. Maybe someone lazy in their warehouse just picked up three faulty dresses without checking. I'm really baffled as to why they will send me incorrect dresses on purpose. And one of the dresses, and a part of one of the dresses had come off that was inside the box. Well it's been well over 24/48/72 hours and no one has responded to my email even though I was told I'll get a response. What a waste of time sending them pictures of the three dresses! So I have no choice but to return the dresses to their shop and get my money back. And the funny thing is that the dress in question has now been reduced! Good luck to the next unlucky person to purchase the dress. Bottom line is don't buy anything from Zara website (their warehouse will give you a faulty item or seconds). It's the second time it's happened to me and I haven't learnt my lesson! No more shopping from Zara again, they don't care about their customers!

review_title: If I could give negative stars I would!
review_text: Ive ordered twice now from Zara and both times my order has not arrived. ( I have ordered in the past without issues)It has then taken numerous attempts for ME to chase THEM when I have been promised call backs that have not occurred. They advise it has been escalated to the courier (who I have been told by one of their CSAs is not reliable) and will take 48 hours for a response. I then contact their customer service line again for them to advise they will escalate. When I advise this has already occurred they dont know what to say and reel off a script. Even the girl on their IVR message sounds like she has given up the will and doesnt want to be there! I love Zaras products but I cannot go through this every time I order. Someone from Hermes is clearly marking the packages as delivered when they havent been!!!! Sort it out Zara!!! At least offer me compensation for the amount of time I have spent chasing you over the last 4 months. Pathetic! Thank goodness you are not regulated by the FCA otherwise you would be out of business pretty sharpish!

Understandably we were only able to take 6 items in at once, but when we asked if we could leave the rest of our items on a rack or with a staff member, we were told it was at our own risk because the clothes were being taken back out. This meant that we had to go in one at a time and hold each others items, so we ended up taking about an hour just trying on items. This also meant a lot of customers were forced to stand in a group outside the changing rooms, causing a lot of problems and confusion. The woman manning the changing rooms was also very rude and fed up. A customer tried to help her by putting her items on the rack, and she moaned and rolled her eyes. She was also very impatient with customers and unfriendly.

Zara Miranda Sydney My daughter received 2 dresses as Christmas presents from her Grand mother unfortunately the sizing was incorrect we drove to our nearest outlet which is 60km from Home only to be told that due to the items being purchased more than 30days age there was no exchange policy and nothing could be done. I have spoken to both consumer affairs and department of fair trading and was told that this is not the case and that the goods should have been replaced. The level was of service I received at your Miranda Sydney store was disgraceful, prompting other customers that were waiting in line to speak up firstly we were told that the store is not responsible for incorrect and poor gift selections and more care should be taken which seems ludicrous considering we were actually very happy with the purchase the issue was sizing which one would think was an understandable mistake when absent Grandparents purchase gifts also we were not looking for a refund we simply wanted to either exchange the items for either the same product or something of equal value On explaining to the store manager that we were given different information from consumer affairs regarding your returns policy the response was If you really think you can take this further and go above by all means give it a go please remember this conversation took place in front of other customers which really isnt a great look it was a really arrogant approach especially when your dealing with a 12 year old girl. This discussion and the fact that my daughter was left unable to exchange her Christmas present which in turn leaves her with no Christmas present obviously upset her to the point that she was in tears when I asked if there was anything that could be done as my daughter is clearly upset the delightful mangers response to her was Its not my fault she is crying its store policy and as said earlier I am not responsible for poor decisions when purchasing gifts I am completely lost to how someone can be so incredibly insensitive. We have purchased many items from Zara before as my childrens Grandparents however after this treat we wont be shopping again I really do think that at Christmas there should be some leeway on the30day policy but even more disappointing if the way that the issue was handled and especially the way my daughter was spoken too. If you are willing to do the right thing and actually show some Christmas cheer and over an exchange that would be greatly appreciated I will certainly be in contact with fair trading and consumer affairs if I havent heard from you however I will not be holding my breath

WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER!!! I made an order from ZARA in October 2017, before I went on a holiday I arranged a return of the unwanted items and a specific date for pick up of this order. My mother was staying in my home and waited in all day for the pick up and no one came to collect the boxes! Once I got back from the holidays I noticed the boxes were not picked up therefore contacted customer services immediately, I was told I would be sent free post returns labels by email and shortly after this my call cut off. I waited for the free post labels to be sent, however nothing came through, I called back to chase this up and they said they could do nothing as the order was out of their returns policy timeframe. I have wasted hours of my time making calls to ZARA,writing complaint emails, tweeting about this etc. And although I was promised a call back from the manager no one ever calls me back, its near impossible to get to speak to a manager they say a manager will call within 72 hours but wont provide any other details. As I mentioned no one calls back. When I called several times in November a number of my calls just cut out abruptly, when I questioned why this was happening they said theyve had problems with their telephone connections. When they informed me that all theyre calls were recorded and they had no record of my calling to return the items over the them, I begged them to listen back on all my calls, especially the ones that cut out after I was promised free post labels. This was one of the problems clearly they have no record of their calls that are cutting out. They said that they spoke to their courier (Hermes I think) and they apologised for not picking up my returned parcels, but there was nothing they could do as its their Policy. Their treatment toward me has reduced me to tears. Essentially I am to suffer the penalty of being unable to complete a returns as their courier never collected the parcels and there were problems with their telephone connections and calls cutting out and them not sending me the free post returns labels, they are a DREADFUL company to deal with. How on earth do they get away with treating their customers like dirt, never in my life have I experienced such poor customer care, inflexibility and rudeness. I wonder who regulates their company if anyone? This company needs regulation immediately, they get away with this appalling treatment toward their customers.

review_text: I will never shop at Zara again. Firstly, their refund policy is practically non existent, secondly their phone operates are just plain rude. I ordered 30s worth of baby clothes online - I dont shop on the shop as I live on a boat and live no where near one! A pair of leggings for just 3.99 came with a tag on and were faulty but I didnt really mind about the fault as they were still wearable. All I wanted was a refund for the leggings as would have to go out of my way to get the tag removed and as I dont drive would have to pay for a taxi to the nearest Zara. I was on the phone for about an hour to be told I will not get any refund and they will not be able to go out of their way to solve my problem. I was told I would have to wait until our boat stumbled across a Zara stor to get a refund. THIS IS ABSOLUTLEY RIDICULOUS. It costs 3.99 just give me a refund and then when I am next in a town which has a Zara I can ask them to take the tag off. They told me this was not possible. I am just happy that it was online a cheap item because if it was over 20 I would have gone absolutely mad!!! Will never shop there again and do NOT recommend anybody use their online service.

review_title: I am really disappointed with zara I Ķ
review_text: I am really disappointed with zara I have shopped online with them quite a few times and always had great service, I purchased two tops in the sale on Monday and normally they would be sent out Monday night delivered on Tuesday, they still haven't left the warehouse yet even tho iv paid for delivery, I contacted zara on chat and was told that over the sale they don't do next day delivery it's 2-6 business days for delivery, strange that when I order two things in the sale last week and it was next day delivery and that was over Christmas, also what has annoyed me is delivery cost for next day is ¬£3.95 and I paid ¬£3.95 but still haven't recieved my order, if they don't do next day delivery during sale time why not have this on their website to tell people and why charge the same delivery costs.

It didn't fit and we don't live anywhere near a store so she returned it to a Zara store on her return to London where she is a student.
