review_title: Not Just Poor Service, Downright Disrespectful
review_text: I would like to start by saying that i was a retail manager for 5 years, I understand what it means to toe the line between excellent customer service and company policies. It's difficult to please everyone. With that experience, I usually am certain about an item before buying to reduce the returns/exchange frustrations and if I am iffy about an item I will keep my receipt and tags on just in case. I bought a few items in the winter sale this past year and there were a few of them that ripped/tore after the first or second wear. I thought, no big deal, I paid sale price for them and didn't keep my receipt. I'll repair them even though they are subpar quality because I didn't keep the tags and receipt. Last week I bought a pair of trousers I tried on and knew that I liked. I took them home, removed the tags and threw away the receipt. I wore them and was pretty happy with them but one of the legs kept twisting and was slightly tighter than the other. I thought the issue was me, so I ignored it. After all, I threw away the receipt and was wearing them so nothing I could do now. However, I went to wear the for the second time (this is before I had washed them at all) and one of the back pockets had ripped and the seam on the other pocket started to come apart as well. I figured with a defective item, no matter if you had the receipt or not (I still have the card I purchased on and can provide bank statement proof of purchase) that I would be able to get a refund. Normally I would also accept an exchange if that's the company's policy, but this was the third item that I purchased and wore once before it started to come apart, so I was pretty annoyed with the downhill quality of Zara's items. The first person at the store I spoke to said nothing can be done without a receipt, even if it's defective. So I asked to speak to a manager who repeated the same thing. It is incredibly UNCOMMON to not be able to look up transaction history with the purchase card, so not being able to return without a receipt is just downright bull. I fussed a little (I hated having to pull that upset customer thing that people did to me all the time, but it was pretty out of the ordinary). The manager caved and said he'd "make an exception and allow me to exchange same day but it will be final sale." I was unhappy at this point because this was the third item I had bought that began falling apart after the first wear. But nonetheless I tried to shop around. Here's where it begins to get even worse. I have extremely low self-esteem and extreme body issues to begin with and when I bought these trousers, they were the only pants in the entire store I tried on that fit me. So making me go back through the store to try on dozens of pants that didn't fit was an incredibly upsetting process for my already weak self-esteem. I felt terrible about myself and disrespected by the staff at this location because they didn't seem to care this was a defective item I had. When I finally found a pair of shoes and some t shirts (not trousers which is an item that I needed), I went up to the register to begin the process. The items I chose to exchange were 11 cents less than the worth of the trousers. The manager informed me that I would have to find another item in order to complete the transaction. I told him that I would've bought more but the store had nothing in my size. There were about 4 other items I chose that didn't have my size available. He said "Sorry but you need to spend at least 11 cents." He didn't seem to care that not only did I have a defective item that I didn't want, but the store didn't have my size in the items I wanted in order to exchange this defective product. What's even worse, is that the employee put a sensor back on the defective pants I was returning, as if they were going to put those pants back out on the floor for someone else to purchase knowing that they were ripped! I said I didn't care about the 11 cents, they can keep it. To which he responded that it would create a register discrepancy and he can't do that. Being a manager at a retail location for 5 years, I know that an 11 cent positive register discrepancy is not a big deal, you just take it out at the end of the night, or better, take out a dime and a penny and leave it on the counter in case someone is short a couple cents at the register later. They refused to complete my transaction, and I left the store with my defective trousers in hand, crying because I was forced to try on dozens of items that did not fit my body when I was already having body image issues. I didn't eat that night, ashamed of my own body and upset at how I was treated by the staff at the Carlsbad location. I then called the customer service to report the issue, and they just reiterated the exact same thing. They did not seem to care that the item was defective, the store didn't have any items I could exchange it with, and I was treated like a piece of garbage. This behavior is endemic within this company now. I was a lifelong customer and was so excited that this store was opening near me. But now, I will never shop at Zara again because of how I was treated. They made me hate myself in that process. This is the most disgusting customer service I have ever had in my life.

I bought men's lace up shoe from Zara @ 3,990/-. I frequently travel interstate and internationally so I requested delivery in my hometown. A week after the delivery I came back home and found the shoe size is smaller than what they have quoted. All my sandals, slippers and other shoe's are of #9, but Zara's size 9 didn't fit my foot, so I went back to their store in person exchanged it with size 10. I wasn't happy travelling 20+ kilometers to get it exchanged as the store manager preferred I visit the store rather exchanging it at my place of residence whenever it was feasible for him as I had no rush to wear the shoes. Anyhow, got the shoe exchanged, came back home only to find one lace being short of other. I had no intention to go back and forth 40kms to talk about lace. I managed to change it and viewed various videos on YouTube to learn knots that will cover the short lace. The shoe appeared to be good and people at home really appreciated my choice. Considering that I thought of purchasing another pair only to find out that the prices were slashed to 1,990/- as the product was now put on sale. A week after that I had to travel internationally so thought this would be a good time to take new pair of shoes so it gives me confidence that they won't break/tear out. On the contrary, the shoe lace started to loosen it's grip, the material on the top got holes, color faded and spots all around the top/front of the shoe. The sole is however un impacted. When approached Zara customer service, initial request was to send pictures, later a call got to me saying it's user fault we don't think our quality standards are poor. We can't replace the item. How is that possible that after a months use, the shoe gets worn out? The customer service says it's the usage of a person. So do they think we put on shoes and play rugby or football with them in offices? Really disappointing to see such a big brand like zara to provide such a cheap service. If all you want is to steal money from others, I better suggest tell your CEO or top officials to beg instead of providing cheap quality, lower standard materials to customers. We'll be more happy donating than being robbed. If the product is not being replaced in a weeks time, they sure are going to get a legal notice from my end as I've seen so many complaints now, I call upon everyone to file a class action so once in for all either the management agrees to our complaints or the judicial system shall close out their business for good so that there are no future white & blue collar robbery's of this sort.

review_title: One star is even too much for thisĶappalling company
review_text: One star is even too much for this company. I have never ever experienced such appalling customer service, they just dont want to know. Its over a week since I placed an order and have not received it. Daily phone calls are a total waste of time. You get fobbed off with the courier hasnt got back, theres no manager here, they dont start till 10.30 am, by 5.30 theres no one there. It dosnt take over a week to get in touch with a courier for an answer. I deal every day with courier services and usually get an answer within 2 hours. Customer services are arrogant and nonchalant, basically they dont give a toss. Zara are so eager to take your money but not to give a refund when a parcel has gone missing. Your the one made out to be a criminal. Ive waited 3 days for a so called manager to ring me back....Im still waiting. I am now going through the claims court to get my money back, and I will make it my mission to inform every social media platform just how bad this company is. DO NOT DO NOT BUY FROM THIS COMPANY. Im utterly disgusted with the way they treat a problem, they are nothing but fraudsters.

I received awful customer service, and made a complaint through resolver, and all they could offer was sorry no compensation. I waited in a queue with 2 toddlers for 20 minutes to find out that I was in the wrong queue for returns, because of their very small signs. I asked them to let me return there, as I didn't want to queue again in another line for 20 minutes with 2 restless toddlers. After pleading with the manager she made us queue again to set an example as appose to reviewing the situation and dealing with it as a separate case, from her generic thoughts, other customers were appalled. Terrible service, rude staff. Waited nearly 10 minutes at men's section whilst 4 staff puzzled over one customer's refund. Even when a Manager came she just added to the throng and continued to ignore the queue of waiting customers. Why couldn't 1 or 2 of them have looked up, acknowledged the queue and moved to another till to serve? Eventually everyone in the queue got fed up and left without purchase. I don't think any of the staff even noticed. Following day went back to speak to a manager to complain - attitude was appalling, instead of an apology or taking anything on board, they simply didn't care. Clearly they're not interested in making sales!

I opened a case via Resolver (RES5482910) over some extremely poor quality clothing. Zara agreed to give me a full refund. I posted the items back at my own cost, via recorded courier so I have proof of them signing for delivery. They have failed to refund me and have suddenly stopped replying to my Resolver messages. I will now be issuing a claim at the small claims court. I spent ¬£250 - ¬£500 in their store but now completely boycotting them over this because they want to jip me out of about ¬£100 worth of the order.

review_title: No shame and no quality of their products
review_text: If I could give, ZERO star. Received a kid jacket on Tuesday night, my son wore it on Wednesday. Zipper broken on same day within 12 hours. How can the quality be this bad? Talked with customer service about exchange or refunds they told me to email to Zara with picture proof. Took the pictures and sent to Zara. The most frustrated and enraged experience with shopping is with this company. They replied me this is not related to manufacture defect and I paid full price for it. The quality is even worse than Joe Fresh by Loblaw. The part that holds and secure the end of the zipper was missing after unzipped in 12 hours???!!!! And this is not manufactured defect??? Dont waste your money is this company.

Absolutely disgusting service and would strongly deter anyone from using them. I placed an online order for click and collect. Received both text and email to say in store. Went in to store to collect waited near on half hour to be told parcel isnt in store and I would have to try again in two days. Contacted customer service via chat TWICE the chat was disconnected. Contacted via WhatsApp explained the situation was told I would be refunded I am STILL awaiting confirmation email and receipt of refund and am now being ignored when I have asked for confirmation that the refund has been processed. I have also asked for their complaints procedure this is also being ignored. I am now without the item I ordered AND without the money I paid for it and will have to spend more time taking this further.

I was give a cardigan as a gift, unfortunately it was massive so I returned it to store but it was two days over their 30 day return policy. Even with the receipt (which I retrieved from the generous gift giver), still in its original bag and still with all the labels attached I was not allowed to swap it for something else. I was not asking for money back just s So look out everyone buying their Christmas presents early this year...DONT shop in Zara as if it doesn't fit your loved ones will not be able to return/swap with or without a receipt...shame on you Zara

review_title: Disappointing online service and nonexistent customer service.
review_text: I've placed a couple of orders through Zara's online store recently and have had defective or items come in less than stellar condition. Mind you the items arrived very fast and wrapped in tissue and the bags came with dust bags. All of this appreciated, but the condition of the pieces is another issue entirely. Decals coming off of a bag, creases in fabric on a backpack, missing belt from trench coat. So far every single item purchased had some kind of issue. This is a warning to be aware of purchasing through their online service. You will only be asked to provide photos. Always always always keep your tags attached if you want to return. Defective items they send can't be returned in store. (I've never tried, just what I've been told from their online chat service) They tell you they will get back to you with resolutions within 24-48 hours and you'll hear nothing back. Their online pricing system is also flawed. Depending on where you live and which site you access, you could be spending $50+ more than other countries on some items. If they continue to operate their business the way that they are, their days are numbered. I've never had to write a review like this before. It's shocking.

review_title: Bought a pair of socks boots
review_text: Bought a pair of socks boots, hardly worn them, the soles are still clean, the other day the heel snapped and I nearly fell over, took them back, but did not have the original receipt of 54.99 Zara refused a refund or any sort of credit note, instead they offered me 7.99 for the reduced price, their customer service is awful, their goods are shoddy and not well made and because of their vast turnover of stock if you've mislaid your receipt youre done for. Customer service is complete and utter diabolical.

Absolutely atrocious. Really wish Id had read some of ZARAs reviews before ordering from them. Made an order in late September for my boyfriends birthday present. According to the courier (myHermes) it was delivered the following day. Its coming close to a month, and still hasnt come! Customer service is a joke, had to ring twice and use the online chat service only be told nonchalantly that theyre looking into it or the case is closed because its been delivered. Still yet to hear an official answer via phone or email, I can only hope that my parcel turns up somewhere around my university. Otherwise thats 60 down the drain. Dont feed into this joke of a retail company, basically daylight robbery! Never stepping foot in or around ZARA again in my life.

Terrible service. I bought a coat from Zara, but changed my mind about it as it was hard to get on (suede inner which stuck to your clothes). Unfortunately I had no receipt as Id bought the coat on holiday and lost track of it. I took it into a store today, two weeks later, with a bank statement showing the date Id bought it, the amount paid, and the company (Zara). Staff refused to look at this proof of purchase, repeating that without a receipt they could not even give me a credit note, let alone a refund. The coat was in perfect condition, still with labels on, in the bag I bought it, and whats more THERE WERE IDENTICAL COATS HANGING IN THE STORE at full price. I rang customer service three times in the shop, on the advice of staff; however it was clear this was just a delaying tactic as customer service were not going to do anything either. I now have a coat I do not want and cannot return. Very very disappointed. I will never be shopping in Zara again.

ZARA is supposedly an international company, but they dont support their product when crossing borders. I purchased several items in Toronto recently. Two of which did not fit or were sold to me damaged. I didnt realize this until I got back home to Houston. Attempting to rectify the exchange/refund at my local store I was made aware of their international policy of not honoring returns or exchanges. Each country is treated like a separate business and after wasting an hour on the phone with the US and Canadian corporate office I realized I was only hitting a brick wall. ZARAs clothes are only nice because they are cheap and dont look half bad for a mall store. I guess you do get what you pay for. Youve proven your loyalty to me,ZARA, so my loyalty to you will be in the form of no longer gracing your showrooms full of cheap knockoffs.

review_text: My 14 years old soon was on a study trip to Poland and wanted to buy something to memories the trip by so he went into a ZARA shop and found this very nice cap. But there was something with the lock; the "very" nice shop assistant explain him what it should be like that and he just should make a not to it. Wrong price label - but you still pay something else Even though they label the items wrong in their shops, they insist you pay the price in their system. Thus us not common practice when the shop makes an error - customer is in good faith and they should be thankful for you pointing out their error

Zara is THE worst clothing store for service, the staff are ignorant, snooty and miserable looking. I feel like they are doing me the favour by taking my money! I recently had an issue over a faulty item, nothing major but I wanted a brand new item to exchange. Large hole in pocket, Zara is famous for buttons falling off, bad lining on coats and holes in pockets. But their fashion is cool! My item had all tags and was less than a week old along with my receipt the snippy Manageress refused an exchange and would only offer a repair. Why not accept? Well, why should I?! I mean its a consumers right to accept it or return it for a new one but to not even get a refund is an abuse of the Goods Act. Its disgusting that Zara are bullying and manipulating people into the belief that they cannot get their money back. Ive contacted my bank (Retail Disputes) who will get my money back, plus Ive contacted trading standards with my complaint. I will never purchase from Zara again, Ive spent thousands over the years on my family and now my teenage daughters shop there. They too are refusing to return. Greedy company!!

review_title: Denim/Elastic blend is of really low quality in mens jeans
review_text: Quality of Zara's male jeans has fallen off a cliff. I used to love Zara clothing and some aspects of the brand (such as jackets), I'm still quite a fan. But your design team needs to understand just how bad the quality of the jeans are and its putting me off buying your products. In the space of 2 years I've had 6 pairs of jeans from Zara. Yesterday, my 6th pair went and I decided I'd had enough. I'll opt to spend double on levis for example from now on as I know I'll get at least double the amount of wear out of them. The first pair ripped (on the inner thigh), second and third wore on inner leg area. I then found a pair I was happy with, the light blue jean. However, in the space of 6 weeks I've had one pair rip and the other the button has come off. I spoke about refunding these to an assistant (without having the jeans with me) and his response was we cannot judge how long we've worn the jean so cannot offer a refund, fair enough. But I myself know how badly made the jeans are, they're Primark quality at three times the price. Expected a lot more of Zara, hoping you raise your standards again soon as I will be shopping elsewhere until you do.

Zara Customer Service is shocking, have been chasing for a refund for an order I havent received since 20th September. Customer service helpline is poor and do not help resolve your issues. I have now rang at least 3 times and all they seem to do is put the phone down on you or put you on hold. I have also spoken to 10 people through the chat service who all give different information and dont seem to have any information from previous conversations. Do not purchase online, as they use couriers like Hermes who are not reliable and always seem to lose your parcels and Zara does not refund you for orders if they are lost.

Solihull Branch Shocking Never Again!! Shocking service, tried to exchange a top with a valid receipt in original bag with tags attached never worn only tried on. Assistant was clearly not interested and said i cannot exchange the top because it had a make-up powder mark on it, she then called the manager. They then basically made me out to look like some sort of criminal and starting telling me they would not except an exchange and i had better not wash it and try returning!! The manager was clearly not interested and just handed me a card for customer services to complain!! and told me they would call security to leave. Very embarrassing never received such terrible customer service before!! Zara Terms and conditions are not true!! Subject to the conditions set forth below, Zara will gladly accept returns or exchanges of new, unworn, unwashed, and unaltered merchandise with all internal garment tags intact with your original receipt within thirty (30) days of the original purchase date.

Very poor service!! I love Zara but will not shop there anymore, I ordered some clip-on earrings from the website, when they were delivered, I removed the packaging to try them on but decided they weren't for me. I sent them back, but received an email correspondence a few days later saying that they would not accept them as the packaging had been torn and they were not fit for resale. The packaging was sealed and could not be opened without tearing it!! All this was pointed out to their Customer Services but to no avail, I cannot believe that they expect you to not try things on so that you don't tear the packaging or that if you do, they don't have spare packaging at their warehouse. As I say, very poor service and it's a permanent goodbye from me!

I have WhatsApped them to flag this up already but they didnt respond to any of my points so Im making them more public on a wider platform, while warning others. Zara do not include any paper in their packages so you are not given any returns label. You have to log back in, process a refund. You have three options 1 - Get courier to come back (no time is given! You get an email that says well come soon?) 2- Download a Royal Mail postage label 3- In store I live miles from a store, work full time so am never home for a courier and dont have access to a laptop or computer so can never download my label. Twice I have sent back at my own cost. I have had to chase this refund up through whatsapping Zara. Also, I queried if they were trying to be eco friendly by not printing anything, why did their cardboard box come wrapped in plastic?! Also, they could give an option of the customer paying 1 for a postage slip in their parcel and donate the money to replanting trees. They wont, as not providing a return label probably discourages people from posting stuff back as its such a rigamoral! Clever from a business point of view. Very FRUSTRATING for your customers! I have not encountered any other online companies do this, from home, food or fashion.
There is bad customer service. They do sometimes have nice things but do not back their products! A waste of money!

review_title: Zara Does NOT value their customers
review_text: Sadly, I recently tried to return clothes that I ordered online to Zara, and they refused the return saying it was just over their strict 30 day return policy. They wouldnt even give me an in store credit. Nothing! They sent me out of the store with my large box I entered with. They told me I needed to call their customer service number and they would tell me what to do. This was on Sunday, and of course their customer service department hours are M-F. Again, Zara not meeting the needs of their customers. If you are willing to take money from your customers 7 days/week, then you should probably support your customers needs 7 days/week as well. To add injury to insult, this trip to SFO was my third, and each time the line was 15 people long, and I could not spend an hour returning the items, which is why I was over the 30 days. I explained this to Zara customer service, and they rudely said Im sorry, the store was open, and you should have waited. I have been a customer of Zaras for years, spending close to $15,000/year. However, until this issue, I have never had to return items. Unfortunately, given that Zara very clearly does not value their customers, or appreciate their patronage, I can no longer support them. I will also be sure to share this information with my extremely large family, friend, and professional colleague circles.

review_text: i always been there but clothes always the same they don't really put a new arrival clothes. and price always high

I never complain about a company online but this felt necessary. I recently was gifted baby clothes from Zara for my baby shower. I returned some items along with a gift receipt through the mail. After about 2 weeks, I called to see if they had received the package and to make sure the credit would be returned to me and not the gift giver (so that she would not know I returned her gift). They read back my email to me and said the credit would be sent there. A week later, I called back and was told the credit was actually emailed back to the gift giver. I awkwardly asked the gift giver to look through her email and she did not see a credit. Three days later, I called Zara back and was then told the credit was sent back to her credit card and there was nothing they could do to get the credit to me. The very, very rude customer service rep told me that he didn't see why I couldn't call the gift giver and ask her to send me the money for her gift. He said he did not know why they didn't see the gift receipt with the package, or why I was told three different stories each time I called. He would not transfer me to anyone else and said that he was the highest person I could speak with and took no ownership for their mistakes. Needless to say, I would not return anything via mail and trust that you will receive the credit, even if you do everything in the instructions to receive it.

We therefore tried to return the dress to the Zara store in the Intu in Derby, it was refused because the return was a few days out of time. When we explained that it was a gift to the rather smug and condescending 13 year old purporting to be the Deputy Store Manager she was unmoved. When we said we would take a voucher she was unmoved. When my daughter started to get tearful about she was unmoved. We asked for their complaints process and were passed a customer services business card which was close at hand (not surprisingly having now read the posts on this website - they must get through a lot of these). This is a commercially illiterate way to conduct your business. Now we will never shop with Zara again, our daughter wont, our friends who bought the item wont and as we relate our story to others they probably wont. All for a refund value of 22.99! Moronic.....

review_title: No REFUND FROM ZARAPlease dont EVERĶ
review_text: No REFUND FROM ZARAPlease dont EVER SHOP AT ZARA! They are thieves and wont refund your money. I bought a suit about a month and a half ago and it was not flattering on me, so I decided to return it. Went in the store, returned it. A few weeks later I check my banks statement there was no credit back from Zara. I called customer service and she told me it takes 14 business days, I said its been 14 days, she said no 14 business days , youre on day 11. I was like ok, Ill wait another 3 days. Another 2 weeks pass by, I get a message from my follower out of nowhere, writing me that she knows I shop at Zara a lot , since Im a blogger and tag them all the time and WARNING me to be careful with returning with them, they ARENT REFUNDING a lot of people. Here I am thinking; oh goodness Ive made a return almost a month ago and have not seen a refund, I hope it wont happen to me, let double check one more time. Nope, no refund. I call again customer service, and guess what they tell me? I owe them money. Like what??? Are you serious??? For? What? She said Ive made a purchase a year ago on a blazer. Ive made a purchase last November of 2017 the blazer was smaller on me, Ive made a request to exchange for size bigger. I sent the blazer back, got the right size delivered. A year later theyre telling me Ive never returned the blazer. But I did. I am so deeply shocked and disappointed. Ive spent thousands of dollars, not even kidding. Ive shopped at Zara for years, Ive had in store experience where they overcharged me for extra things at least 3 times Ive never gone back to claim it, and they are treating me like a thief when they are at fault. I told her why in the world am I hearing about this right now? How many refunds I didnt receive back from my returns now that I think about it. Ive made a few returns within this year and thinking did I ever get my refund back?????? Probably not. Sometimes I dont check , I just assume I did. Lesson learned. Always check people, specially with ZARA! She told me to get a refund I need to give them proof of my return and label. I found the label, but its no longer in system. I called usps who I returned with and they said they dont have anything in system after 120 days :( so here I have to waste my time applying and wasting my time on something I feel is not my fault. How in the world am I supposed to dig after a year? Why not tell me that ASAP? Zara is at fault at this, specially for not informing me they never received my package. But thats what they are doing to hundreds of people. Ive talked to some people and they said Zara says they never received the package and dont refund them money. Even my close friend is having exact same experience. She told me she returned something a month ago now and didnt receive the refund. How do we know its not them up yo this??? Zara truly has the worst customer service. With years Ive shopped with them, if I got a faulty item or anything they never helped. Worst worst . They said they will call back but never did. Im praying my $150 will be refunded. Zara can screw people over, but not for long, there is always karma. Ill keep posted

review_title: Fingers out of online shop! Terrible customer service!
review_text: I ordered some pants on Black Friday to be delivered on store. The delivery was delayed and I would not be in Berlin to pick it up. I went to the store and found the same trousers available there. I bought it without the discount of Black Friday, since I wanted it for my trip. Talking to the customer service, they told me I would be able to cancel the purchase through them, but once and only once it arrived on store. I accepted the conditions, although frustrated for having to deal with this 10.300km, one ocean and 3 hours timezone away. Once I finally managed to organize myself to make a paid call from Skype during the working time in Germany, the attendant told me I can only resolve this in person once I am back in Berlin. I will be back 6 weeks after the product has arrived in the store. She talked to the boss and told me though, that it will be resolved. I asked for an email to confirm that, since I have already received a wrong promise from them the last time. She sent me this: "Wenn Sie wieder in Deutschland sind, melden Sie sich bitte wieder bei uns per Telefon um die Angelegenheit zu klren." Asking for a confirmation that I will be allowed to return the product even passing the return time, she told me we can only dicuss the terms once I am back. I was 30min on the phone with them paying a Skype call. I had ordered two pants in different colors and size to choose from, and won't need any of them, since I've already tried and bought on store the one I wanted, for 5Ǩ more expensive than the online order. I had a similar issue with other stores because of the delay on delivery due to Black Friday and the close date of my trip, and NONE of them put that much complication on presenting solutions. In fact most of the stores that deliver on branch or delivery points do so, that the package is automatically returned after 10 days of not being picked up. Fimgers out of Zara online shop! Terrible service, over complicated system.

I ordered a jumper as a Christmas present for my mum on Tuesday 18th December, and paid for next day delivery so it was due to arrive on Wednesday 19th December. I received an email to say the item had been dispatched straight away and would be with me by Wednesday 19th. The jumper doesnt arrive on Wednesday so I do an online chat with someone who explained due to high volumes of orders this is why I didnt recieve the item the next day but it should be with me Friday between 8am - 1pm and gave me the tracking number of my item. Why Zara offered next day delivery when they cant fufill this at this time of year still baffles me. 2pm on Friday the item still hasnt been delivered so I track the item on Royal Mail and they havent even received my parcel from Zara so I dont understand why Zara sent an email to say theyve dispatched, and I dont know why Zara said I should receive the item Friday morning at the latest. No parcel arrives Friday morning so I then call Zara asking whats happened with my parcel and got through to an extremely snotty rude customer service member of staff who then tells me the item I ordered is low in stock and they might not have it but they can give me a refund. I explain there was no low stock mentioned online when I ordered the item and as its a Christmas present I want the item sent to me ASAP. I asked to speak to a manager and was told its a 24-48 hour call back from a manager. I called today at 12pm to be told its not been 48 hours yet, (I was 3 hours early!!) the manager should call me back today if not it will be a call back on the 26th which is then a 72 hour call back?! So again not sticking to their word. And I now dont have this present to give to my mum on Christmas. So all in all I am highly disappointed with Zara as I could of ordered the same item through a more reputable company and received the item in time for Christmas. Never ordering or shopping here again. Would recommend to avoid this shop.

I must spend 5000 a year in Zara and the device just gets worse and worse. Too many times to speak about. I use the Telford store and Im sure they hire people who are rude on purpose.

The customer service was horrible to say the least. Manager Pedro in Disney Springs was rude and unhelpful. They had me step out of the line to call their customer service number, while carrying my baby on my arm just for them to tell me they couldnt do anything, not even a store credit! Needless to say I will not be buying from them again. And compared with other stores, like for example Bloomingdales which has a return policy of 365 days, it is beyond me the lack of care for their customers!

I ordered a scarf and jacket! ( mens) unfortunately the scarf was a totally different colour to what I had ordered! I tried numerous times to contact via both the WhatsApp contact and the 27x7 contact. Oh my god, what a waste of my time. One week later, a guy arrives to pick up the order in question without any contact at all! No e mail! No whats app! No message and no phone call! Surprise..... Ok, absolutely useless but its in hand now! Couple of days later the useless company deliver the same incorrect scarf... wow! Then I try and sort it out over the phone! Big mistake. Young lady, very polite and promising, but yes! Again, around a week or so goes by (was promised the latest would be two days) and I get a phone call.. hiya, Zara here. Unfortunately the scarf isnt available anymore Can we just refund you No explanation... Terrible,terrible,terrible service. When I actually managed to get through to them. Never again! It also looks as though they cannot be bothered to reply to any bad feedback, a shame really. As they need as much help as they can get!

There was only one fur coat out on the racks that we could actually see and we wanted to buy this coat as it was lovely. However there was so many threads falling out of it we had to pull some out ourselves but clearly others had worn this a lot. We spent ages in the que and then asked the woman the till whether there would be anymore out and about or if theyve got any in the back. She seemed frustrated, cocky and rude at the fact we simply wanted a coat that hadnt be worn so many times and wasnt looking so tatty. We asked politely and she responded there could be one in the changing rooms maybe or somewhere in the store you can go have a look and gave a sarcy smile. She was pouting her lips and giving such attitude and normally anywhere else they would go ask a member of staff to go find the clothing for you instead of you going yourself. We really wanted the coat but the store is massive and has two floors and clothes were thrown everywhere so we werent going to look ourselves. She gave a sarcy and rude response when we said we wont bother buying it then and no offer for a discount was given.

review_title: Zara bad on line service experience
review_text: I bought my daughter a Zara gift voucher on line do Christmas. When shes was unable to activate the card I phoned customer support. What a waste of time that was. When the person I spoke to (Bimla) could not help I asked to speak to her Manager but she refused. Told I would be contacted in 2 days. They are quick to take my money but not to deliver the service. Dont use this company they are useless and have no concept of customer service. Been on phone and e-mail all day. Someone phoned to say the card had been activated but when my daughter tried to make an on line purchase it still did not work!! These people are just not interested. Have requested a refund but no response.

I know its just after the holidays but cmon. Ive just had the most awkward shopping experience of my life in Zara. The staff were so rude and unhelpful. Im not after above and beyond service on a busy day, I have worked in retail and know how hard it can be. That being said, its not hard to be polite and make a difference to someones day by delivering good service. My boyfriend spent an awful lot of money using his gift vouchers from the past year, I certainly wont be giving them any more.

Just look at all the other Zara customer reviews on this website that talk about nothing else other than the terrible customer service dished out by this third-rate company! Here's another disgraceful Zara experience that relates to their Granada store in Spain: The rudest Spaniards I've ever met come from Granada and most of those apparently work at Zara. After being ignored, bumped and bashed by staff wheeling trolleys around the store, with no "excuse me" or apology, we finally made it to the check out intending to buy Ǩ400 worth of clothes. We waited for 15 minutes with just one other customer in front of us before we finally gave up, dumped the clothes on the counter and left. We spent our money at another clothes store 100 meters away where they take customer service seriously. Zara staff are totally unprofessional, obnoxious and devoid of basic manners. We won't be going back. EVER!

Awful rude customer service operative and called back by a equally rude patronising manager. I made an online purchase of Christmas presents inadvertently 2 days before the extended Christmas returns policy and when I tried to exchange them in store (the items were still on sale in the store) I was told this was not possible as I purchased them 2 days too early for their extended returns. This was not made clear at purchase and I dont know any other store which would not exchange goods purchased in the run up to Christmas. Customer services were unhelpful and the manager who called back asked sarcastically if I was aware of their VERY STRICT returns policy and was extremely rude and dismissive. Im an NHS manager and if I spoke to people in that way I would loose my job! Absolutely disgusting service.
Very bad and unorganized customer service and had a very bad experience with a claim I made to them

If I could give no stars and leave it with 0 I would. They have the worst customer service. The customer service department are horrible, very unprofessional and unhelpful. Will never purchase from them, especially online

Discusting customer service both in store and over the phone. Received clothes for my children for Christmas. No receipt kept unfortunately. Tags still on,unworn. Went into the white rose store in Leeds to swap all for one size bigger. Looked before hand and the items were still in stock in the correct size. Went to the desk to be told that they do not offer exchanges with out a receipt. Explained that I only needed one size up but was told no and given a card for head office. I could understand if I was asking for my money back. But only wanted a size up of the identical clothes. Not happy, will not be shopping at Zara again.

review_title: So much so I love Zara clothes and buyĶ
review_text: So much so I love Zara clothes and buy often their customer service is not something worth talking about. Will not take clothes without receipt even if just exchanging and sizing issue. If item is damaged and you have no receipt then also you are in trouble. Staff in most stores look lost and you be lucky to get a smile out of them. sometimes I think Zara is experimenting on putting human robots in their stores. The above are consistent issues but my reason for writing the review is and online order placed on 10 December. Visit store on 14 and 21 December, no sign of order. wont check where is it held. No apologies. Get handed a number for customer service which is a 0800 number , tried calling and was on hold for 5-7 minutes after which I gave up on wasting my money. Since 21 December till date today 2 Jan 2019 I have been in contact with customer service via whats app. All I get to hear is my query has been escalated and someone will contact me in 24-48 hours, nothing !! I emailed the given customer service team to escalate it who have not even acknowledged my email leave aside dealing with it. I don't know what sort of customer service is that where you take peoples money and then forget about it and get busy getting rid of your old stock in Christmas sales. JOKES! Update 2 /1/2019- I have been informed a refund will be issued to me in 10-14 days, which will take the whole drama length to 1 month where they have been holding my money. will they accept it that next time I buy something from Zara I ask them for 10-14 days to pay for the item so how is possibly acceptable to take 2 weeks to issue a refund . When asked for reason I was told its Zara policy, who wrote policy, when was it informed to me and how can they get away with it. No apologies , no gesture to keep customer service..

review_title: Shocking customer services from CHLOE in Swansea Store!!
review_text: Shocking customer services, especially from a manager, Chloe, in Swansea store. Christmas gift brought 14th November, manager stated 15th Novemeber for Christmas returns policy-we were not told this. Item had the receipt and wanted and exchange-not refund-on 2nd January. Chloe said there is nothing we can do as I am 2 days out. The customer was not stated this when purchasing items but Chloe said its on the receipts-after you have purchased??? Extremely stone cold attitude to have. I phoned customer services and Josephine stated that it is up to the Managers discretion. Upon return she refused to do anything as of the dates. I will be contacting consumer direct and seeking legal advice as Zaras is a huge franchise-like River Island, John Lewis etc who have excellent policies may I add. I have been management in retail before and would waver this as customers keep people in jobs and the high street going. No wonder firms are closing is customers are treated like this.

Have been advised I will not get a refund or exchange. The reason being that I am out of the time limit, because they did not send a courier to pick up the parcel. according to them this is my fault! Rude customer service. This is the first and last time I will have anything to do with this firm. How are they so popular? Can't see it lasting much longer with this sort of service.

If I could put a negative star rating I would! One will just have to do since I cant select -1. I have been shopping at Zara in 4 different countries for over 10 years. I tend to go directly to the shops but have recently ordered online in the UK. I enquired about a delivery I had not received. I have never had any issues in the past with any online orders from different retail brands I have ordered from. I had order a few months back from Zara online & received a delivery without issues. However this time I did not receive my online order & there was no delivery note left in my mailbox to collect at the post office. I went on my online Zara account to discover that it had been delivered but where was the package? I was at work that day so I knew I had not collected it. I live in a block of flats therefore if the postman cant deliver it they usually leave a note for collection at post office. After lengthy back and forth Whatsapp messages I was told that there was nothing they could do as I had not selected the signed delivery option. I was informed that the package had been left on the porch. I queried why when there was no instruction to do so, no proof that it had been delivered, as there was no delivery note and that leaving an order on a 2nd floor of a block of flats was not secure delivery. Therefore why leave it where anyone walking on that floor can grab it. I spend thousands of pounds every year shopping at the Zara stores. For the sake of around 32.00 they choose to say there is nothing they can do! They did not offer to resend the items or to give a gift voucher for the value of my order. I am upset but most of all shocked at their response to do nothing! They have lost a loyal customer and I will be sharing this negative experience with family and friends. Shame on you Zara online delivery department! Veronique Fletcher, St Albans, UK.

review_title: Today i went to their store i bought 2Ķ
review_text: Today i went to their store i bought 2 pairs of jeans and when i got home i saw that they were ripped, how can someone pay so much for ripped jeans!??!?!?!

I would like to make a complaint. I ordered some clothes online using 2 zara gift cards. I wanted to exchange some items so I came into the Westfield Stratford branch today and stood in the exchange que for 40 minutes only to be told I was not able to exchange my items even though I had the online receipt and gift cards. They told me I would have to wait 3 days for the money to go back onto the gift card. Then i could use it to buy the items. So I had to buy the items using an alternative method of payment. This is ridiculous I returned Zara items to a Zara shop using a Zara gift card and I had the recipt, why would I then need to wait 3 days before I was able to exchange to the items I wanted. Nowhere on your exchange policy does it say it's 3 day process???? I could understand if I returned the items by post but I brought the items back to the shop why would it take 3 days to transfer the money back to the gift card.

review_title: Don't buy Christmas Presents from Zara
review_text: Please don't buy anything from Zara that you intend to give as a Christmas present. This year they only gave you to the 30 December to return or exchange gifts - do they not realise that some people do not go to the shops after Christmas and between New Year as they are visiting and spending quality time with family and friends and trying to go to the shops is not an option!! Let alone mothers who have double buggies (which is always a struggle to shop with in the first place) and would find it difficult to get around the shops during the peak time of people shopping in the sales after Christmas! Not a nice experience, they always feel as if they get in peoples way as it is, let alone when the sales are on :(

review_title: Wish it could be zero stars
review_text: Wish it could be zero stars. I am a long-time customer of Zara, and spend lots of money there between myself, and my daughter. Placed an order in early December with many items intended as Christmas presents. There were a few items that didn't work out. Since the pieces weren't gifted until end of December, it wasn't until one day after the 30-day return period that I initiated return process. They absolutely would not budge on a ONE-DAY grace period. All the items still in season and full price on the website. I took the request all the way to the top, with not concession. Not even a gift card. I will NEVER do business with a company with such poor customer service.

Went to return a coat that was brought as a Christmas present and because it was 6 days out of the returns period they refused to do any thing so now Im left with a coat and 70 down the drain as it doesnt fit staff in store were rude and staff on the customer service help line were rude wont be shopping here again think Ill stick to next!!!

I purchased items from Zara in December. In the app I requested a shipping label. Their app didn't send me a shipping label. I contacted their customer service within the next few days. The service rep state he would send me at shipping label which was agian not received. I contact Zara and they notified me that they would not take the clothes back since it was now 30 days past return policy. I should not be held accountable for their employees not doing their job. Never shopping at Zara again. Thank god Chase has by back they were able to refund me the full amount of the purchase.

review_title: Worst customer service ever received and wrong item .
review_text: Worst customer service ever received. Ordered the item on online 2 weeks before receivied the item and was completely wrong item.(got 2 dresses Instead of my coats).when called customer service they were not of any use.I returned the wrong item and ask them to send my orders,and after 2 week they had email be the item is out of stock. Staff members extremely unhelpful. Will never go with them again. Worst experience ever. Won't recommend

Worst customer service ever, I bought some items more than 2 months ago and I've never received the items. I contacted them and all they keep saying is that they will come back to inless than 24h as my case is being investigated...Well, this is been going on for 2 months now and they still keep saying they will come back to me in 24H time.
Bad online customer service and very bad delivery service always late dispatch and delays

review_title: Avoid buying anything online from Zara.com
review_text: Avoid buying anything online from Zara, their customer service is atrocious. They have admitted to losing an order I placed, and over three weeks later I am still fighting with customer service to get a refund. They say yes, we will give you a refund within 48 hours, and nothing happens, so you are back to square one to explain your story to another customer service rep, who in turn, tells you to expect a refund within 48 hours. So this is now 4 times I have gone through this explaining and waiting. Today, Flora on their chat, ended our conversation halfway and blocked me, which is extremely unprofessional. So you have to start over again, and no help at all.

review_title: Disgusted with Zaras Online Customer Service
review_text: I am disgusted with Zaras online customer service. I ordered a dress online and when i opened, the dress had tonnes of threads hanging of it and the stitching was not finished. Although i got in the sale, for a dress that rrp at 69.99, the condition was awful. I contacted the 24/7 help section on the app straight away. The lady said the team would review it internally to see whether its faulty or not and i would hear back within 48 hours. I wanted a replacement but was worried due to stock levels being low and she said they will review that too. I didnt hear from anyone within 48 hours so i called them on 05/01 and was told the team had not looked at my case, which is disgusting, there was no excuse for this, but the lady would mark it as urgent for them to see and i would receive a call back on the same day. Didnt hear anything until the 07/01/19, when a woman rang and told me they had deemed the item as faulty. She had told me it was out of stock, and she SPECIFICALLY stated to me that due to this, i would receive a full refund which would be processed straight away and would take 24-48 hours to reach my bank. I wasnt told anything else, just that. I asked to speak to a manager but noone was available so the phone call ended. I then received a random phone call from them on the 08/01, and they told me that the information on the phone was wrong, i would not be receiving a refund UNLESS i sent the item back, which wasnt what i was initially told. I asked to speak to a manager who told me this was a human error, given no compensation for all of the hassle they caused, even though it was their fault..they would not honour what they had first told me, neither do a partial refund. Seriously Zara!? You make millions per year, to compensate a customer 40, does not affect the company whatsoever. I am fuming that i have been messed about, and lied to, and i will never shop online at Zara again. Please look into your customer service Zara, because when retail starts failing due to more and more people buying online, your company is going to struggle a lot due to loss of customers. Keeping your customers happy should always be priority.

Do not buy Christmas gifts from this shop! I received a Christmas present, with a gift receipt, and not realising it was only valid until the 30th December, was mortified when they wouldnt exchange or do anything about it. I have been to the Bristol branch 3 times and queued, after waiting and waiting, decided to return when it gets quieter, I think the queues were for the same reason, unhappy customers unknowingly being refused any exchange or refund for their gifts. After speaking to the manager, still refused to do anything about it, asked for head office number, who will not budge either! This is disgusting Zara! Will never shop with u again, and will tell all, about your disgusting policy! Disappointed and upset to say the least. Shame on you. Rude staff throughout!

review_title: By far the worst experience Ive everĶ
review_text: By far the worst experience Ive ever had shopping Instore after asking why I was being asked to pay nearly 15 more for an item in the U.K. then I would if I was in Europe. I picked an item up that was priced at 29.99Ǩ so thought wont be more then 25-30. When I got to the till after waiting 20 mins I was told the item was 39.99. When o asked why seeing as the Euro to pound is normally cheaper or at worst the same. When I asked for an explanation a manger came who told me it was due to import duty. Once I pointed out that in fact we are still in the Eu so we dont pay import duty he looked at me blankily and in front of my family asked me to leave or he would have me removed. I was shocked and emailed Zara customer service who ignore me for 2 weeks and then called me and actually asked what the issue was as they hadnet even bothered to read what Id written and this was by all accounts a manger! 6 weeks this went on for promised call backs nothing happened not even an apology. I for one wont set foot in another Zara and Id advise you dont either

I bought a parka less than 2 months ago, the quality is TERRIBLE!! The zipper doesnt work anymore & the fillings get all over my sweaters or any top that Im wearing!! This is the worst purchase worth $140! The original price was &200 & I got it on Black Friday! Id shoot myself if I paid full price for this garbage!!

This was my first (and most definitely the last) time purchasing something on Zaras online store. I ordered a coat and upon receiving it, tried it on right away. Due to their extremely weird sizing, the coat did not fit and I ended up having to return it. About a week later, I received an email stating that the item is not in returnable condition. I called to ask what that meant and why they were unable to accept the return. I asked to speak to a manager because there was no way there was anything wrong with the coat I had returned and the online staff member was being rude. The managers response was that there was human hair on that coat which deamed it unreturnable then stated the coat would be shipped back to me. After calling several times, as there was no action being taken on their end, it took over a month for them to ship the coat back to me. This was the worst online shopping experience I have ever had. I will never use Zaras online website in the future. They also shipped and shoved the coat in a small amazon box.

review_title: Poor poor customer service - never shopping online again!
review_text: I am very disappointed in the customer service I have received on my order. Worst experience ever! I ordered a pair of Paperbag Trousers With Shimmer in an M on December 20th 2018, which was never the order I received. Instead, I received a dress which I've never seen before and when I went to check the status on the trousers, it was registered to have been delivered. Therefore, I drew to the conclusion that there had been a mix-up of two orders, so I got someone else's delivery. I then began to get in contact with you (since December 20th!!) to try and get my refund. I have endless times gotten in contact with you via your chat service provided on your website and on January 2nd, I received an e-mail confirming that a courier would be with me within 24-48 working hours. However, 11 days later - I still have the parcel and I have tried to get an update on when I can expect my delivery to get picked up. Since January 2nd, I've been in contact with your customer service 2-3 times online via the chat provided on your website, where I've been told twice that the courier would soon be with me. However, I was expecting the order to be picked up Thursday 10th - no one showed up and I then got in contact with you again to be told that the courier would be with me the next day - which he failed to be. Very very disappointed and frustrating as I have gone through so much trouble already and I haven't even been provided with my refund yet.

Would happily give no stars however this isn't possible on trust pilot! Appalling customer service and customer shopping experience. The majority of retailers I use offer an extended returns policy over the Christmas period but not Zara. Customer service in Bristol store was not a priority as when I spoke to the store manager she directed me to use the website. Not wishing to cause a scene in store I returned home (40 mileround trip) and tried to contact Zara via website chat. Not helpful and was advised to return to the store where purchased? Zara I really think you need to get your act together and understand your customers are keeping your business afloat. I for one will donate my purchase to a worthy charity and vow never to shop at Zara again. Shame on you Zara!

review_title: Wouldnt have even given one star if IĶ
review_text: Wouldnt have even given one star if I could have. Bought Christmas gifts on 6th December. Bought the items in kids till because quieter till. Asked the girl if they had extended return time because of Christmas and she said they did. I dont remember if she said a date or anything but thought Id be back if anything wrong before normal return date was up. I had an awful cold and no store near where I live so was 11th when I went back with three items for a refundThis was at the Westfield store in Shepherds Bush . I was refused a refund. Told it was past their refund window. I explained what Id been told but they were completely uninterested . I asked for the manager and this 30 something man, manager of menswear apparently but totally lacking any politeness or customer service appeared. He took my receipts went off somewhere and came back but said he would be unable to do anything. I told him it was ridiculous that you couldnt bring Christmas presents back. He replied that I had 30 days. I pointed out that usually you buy a Christmas present for someone and give it to them on 25th December! He just kept repeating that you have 30 days. Think he might not have understood what I was saying. Im SO furious. I have 60 worth of clothes which are not any good. I spent about 300 there during December and cant believe they have such a stupid policy over Christmas. Told my daughter about it and she said everyone knows their clothes are ok but their customer service and staff are c... wont be going there again
Shocking ordered daughters shoes they sent 2 odd shoes? Then took 5 different methods of contacting to speak to them shocking service will NEVER USE AGAIN!

I too would have left no stars if possible. My children were bought 2 items for Xmas that are way too small. My daughter is 6 and the size bought was a 6 but the leggings wouldnt even go over her thighs. Tried to return to store today at Sheffield Meadowhall and the customer service was awful. The manager told me that I had until the 30th Dec to return the items, Im sorry but this is a Xmas gift that isnt true to size anyway you are not giving customers enough time to return after the Christmas period. I too will NEVER use ZARA again due to principle. They think they are being clever with the policy but in the long run they will loose more customers. I will certainly be leaving bad reviews on every site possible. Lots of places have policies in place and sometimes these policies can be broken on discretion of the management, the managers need to grow a pair and use their common sense sometimes. Too many jobs worth people working at Zara for my liking. Rant over!!

review_title: Worst customer service I have had in years
review_text: Order a coat waited till 3 days after the delivery date and still not arrived. Call there customer support they told me to wait 24-48 hours for an update. Waited this time heard nothing so contacted them they told me to wait another 24-48 hours still nothing so called and spoke to a manger they told me that it was now a priority case but had to wait 24-48 hours and they would contact me. Waited 72 hours they did contact me and told me the parcel had been lost so was offered a refund or replacement. Still wanting this coat I said a replacement but when would it be with me. They told me they couldnt give me this information it takes 24-48 hours. I demand to speak a manger which took a lot of arguing finally got through and was told they didnt actually have the item in stock and they could only offer a refund and then had the phone put down on me. Service was utterly disgusting would never use this company again.

ok lets start by saying every review ive read off here has been exactly like my experience no product delivered to my house and waiting time was a f...... joke 23 days i waited and within that time i called numerous times even spoke to someone online chat and cussed them so bad they took me off chat this company is ajoke never again will i be ordering from here and i want to expose them because something isnt right here there trying to scam something or another anyway f.... u zara

Well all the reviews relate to my own experience !!!! Some 19 days after returning an item and 3 lengthy phone calls chasing my refund I am still waiting ! I was advised on each phone call I would be contacted between 24 - 48 hours ??? My first phone call was 12 days ago (still waiting ) my last phone call was 96 hours ago (still waiting) I have sent 3 emails also. Quite unbelievable really and I will not make any other online purchases (this was my first experience !!) totally unacceptable and worth no stars whatsoever!!!!!!!! I also provided proof of postage for returned item and again Im still waiting for acknowledgment !!!

If there was a negative rating I would have given that. I ordered few items 12 days ago and the product reaches the location 5 days later and I see no update. So I contact the customer care and they said it will be delivered within the time period. I wait until 12 days later I see an update that it's cancelled as I dint respond. What a pathetic service and I immediately contact the courier and the merchant and all they have to say is you can reorder the item if you like. 12 days I wait for the product and it's cancelled and I have to wait for a refund again and they just have to say I have to reorder again and go through all this. Most pathetic service ever, and there is no escalation level of support either

Really, really poor quality! Even though they have convinced me of the quality and workmanship of their products long time ago, every now and then I still make the mistake to order from Zara. In general, the prices are much too high for what they can offer. Everything I ordered from them, either broke or had a flaw or had a really long time for delivery. It is really unnacceptable how they treat their customers. So, the last experience I had, was today. Last week I ordered an oversized shirt from them, as it was on sale, but still not very cheap. I received it yestarday, so today I wore it for the 1st time, for 8 hours. I would like to mention again that the shirt is oversized. So, after the 8 hours at work, I got home and noticed that the fabric broke, more like torn apart, at the back width armhole stitch. Since the shirt is big on me and there is a lot of extra space, I couldn't have broke it because it s too small for me or anything like that. Also, my work consist in computer work, so again, I did not do any moves to cause the breaking. But this is totally ridiculous. A product from them cannot resist even 8 hours. So far all the products I got from them, broke and got damaged. Thing that never happened at H&M( and they are even cheaper) So, all in all I am never buying from Zara again.

Swansea store horribly rude staff, absolutely no customer service skills,long wait to pay then assistant eye rolling when I asked the price of a perfume on the counter,which I was looking to add on to purchase,there are so many stores with better clothes and much better experience when shopping why would you go there,the few items I did buy turned out to be poorly made that which does not last after couple of washes.Avoid this store.
Disgusting customer service. Possibly the worst Ive ever experienced

review_title: Worst customer experience I have ever encountered with a retailer
review_text: Worst customer experience I have ever experienced. The issues with returns due to a courier not turning up and to then be told its now outside returns period and refund will not be issued! Despite me calling to let them know the courier did not turn up and then to be told its out of returns period and they will not issue a refund! My initial refund request was made before the refund end period so why am I being penalised for a courier service they offer for collection which did not happen?This has caused so much distress and inconvenience. I have never been treated in such an appalling manner by any other retailer. Im disgusted to say the least.

review_title: Zara has THE worst customer service outĶ
review_text: Zara has THE worst customer service out of any retailer. From the second you walk through a shop front all staff are rude, unhelpful and condescending. Returns are a nightmare and faulty goods dont even get acknowledged. I honestly dont know how they stay in business in the UK.

Simply do not respond to emails or messages. Clearly do not care about customer service or retaining loyalty. If you need anything from after sales then good luck because my experience is that they aren't interested.

I dont feel Zara customer service even deserves 1 star! As much as the clothes are lovely thats where things end. Once Zara gets your money you have no chance of seeing it again! BUYER BEWARE!!!! You have to jump through hoops to get an exchange or return. MAKE SURE YOU HAVE THE ORIGINAL BANK CARD or they will NOT even give you a gift receipt. Ridiculous even with the original receipt AND tags on all the clothes. Honestly I NEVER encountered such bad customer service. I could understand a store that carries multiple brands like Nordstrom being hesitant to return because item could be from anywhere but all ZARA items are labelled ZARA. Unbelievable. Not willing to return with receipts and store tagged merchandise without original bank card to have a store gift card not even cash. RIDICULOUS!!!!!!!!!!!

review_title: Too tight in hips area (being that myĶ
review_text: Too tight in hips area (being that my hips are just normally proportioned, not big), cups are too far from each other and leave a big cleavage. Advice is to get a bigger size and tailor it to fit your figure. It also arrived with a stain.

review_title: If I could give this company NO stars, I would!

I tried calling their corporate line and was told I would get a manager to return my call and guess what? Never got a callback. This is so unacceptable. review_title: Why am I forced to give Zara 1 starĶ
review_text: Why am I forced to give Zara 1 star when I want to give 0. Its not fair! This following review is a one year old now and I will give further update at the end. 12/23/17 I have been to a Zara store today in Covent Garden had the most frustrating experience ever and in the end, they made me look like a fraudster. I bought a ¬£30 gift card and after paying the lady who served me, I walked a yard away from the till on my way to leave the store and when I opened the little box, there was no card inside. I came back to the till straight away without leaving the store and complained. The lady looked very confused and I asked them to check CCTV. After 20 minutes they came back out saying that she did put the card inside the box but she hasn't. They wanted to make me believe in that evidence but they wouldn't want to believe in me when I said that she made a mistake. She never came back out and she knows, they know she made a mistake. The security said she would have to pay double if she made a mistake so that's probably why they want to keep on her side and claim she did out the card inside the box. I would never make such a fuss for ¬£30, it's about the principle, she made a mistake just won't admit it. I asked them to show me the evidence but they said for Data protection they couldn't. That data is about me for God's sake. I called the police but hey were useless saying it's a civil matter. If I had committed a crime they would call the police and the store would even check all the cameras,l but why would they not check the cameras to see whether I took the card out of the box, hid it and went back to ask for another one?? They did not check other cameras and they were very unhelpful and at one point rude. I am taking this further but I am sure they would never go against the member of staff. I'm very annoyed as I am an honest person and they basically told me I was lying to them. They even suggested that maybe I was pick pocketed but how?! There was no one around me, I was inside the store and away from the till for 10 seconds and there was a couple of cameras above me. .. If they have nothing to hide why won't they show me the CCTV where they claim shows the staff putting the card inside the box?! I have been a customer for many years now but they treated me like a thief. It's probably safe to buy from them as I have been for a long time but one day... Update ** I have given up on this as they are not worth it and I couldn't put up with the hassle no more. I would have to file a complaint in court etc etc I just don't have the time. I have another friend who gave a gift card worth ¬£80 as a birthday gift to her mate and this happened : the birthday girl went to Zara and bought something worth ¬£20 or so. She never asked how much there was in the card, she just used it to pay. The member of staff KNEW the card had ¬£80 worth of credit in it so instead of returning the card straight away, she ASKED if the customer if she wanted the card back or if she wanted to bin it. Now we normally don't need empty cards back so she said she didn't need it. She only found out the real amount in the card as she went to thank the person who gave her the gift card and her friend was surprise when she said she only bought a small item given the amount of credit she had in the card. We were all shocked. The member of staff got away with ¬£50. The only reason this girl got her money back after some real hassle is because her husband is a police officer and you know, in don't need to tell you the rest. I was stolen by ¬£30 gift card from a member of staff in my own experience so, do not trust Zara. I have decided not to buy from them ever again. I've kept that promise for a year now and I will keep for the coming years. Never again!

review_title: I wish I could give 0 stars....pathetic service for online exchange
review_text: I wish I could give 0 stars! DO NOT ORDER WITH THEM ONLINE! I placed 3 separate orders which qualified for delivery and was sent all of them together in 1 box. I then wanted to exchange 2 items (which had 2 different order numbers). I requested for it online and never received any information about how to arrange for a courier to pick it up. Contacted them on zara chat and after spending an hour on chat which was me replying followed by a 10minute absence from the zara rep and me replying straight away followed by their absence again! Finally a courier arrived, I packed the 2 items in the same box that they arrived and sent them back. Contacted Zara several times to let them know that both orders are in the same box. Twice I was timed out due to inactivity on zara chat (apparently I am meant to just sit and stare at my screen whilst the zara rep disappears for ages to do who knows what as they come back asking questions I had just answered!). After 3 tries I am finally told it has been noted and its fine. Then had to chase them up twice after to check if they had received it. Finally get confirmation that it has been received after 2 weeks. But now I am fault because I sent the 2 items in the same box and did not follow the procedure correctly. Honestly, was I meant to conjure up a zara box for returns out of thin air??? Absolutely pathetic that I am being blamed for their pettiness! If it was that important that the orders stayed separate, then send them to me in separate boxes! Absolutely appalled. If you want to go through hours of this just to blamed for their stinginess, order from them. Otherwise save yourself from this hassle. Ive never had a problem in store, but their online service is downright disgusting.

The email also stated I had not been charged for the original purchase, however my emails to them contained proof that I had been charged for 4 x the total amount due. I contacted my bank January 18. The teller advised me to dispute the transaction online, which I have. Still waiting on final decision. Zara have not contacted me so far for a refund. 2020 UPDATE: I forgot about this review. I ended up getting a charge back with the bank ages ago. They appear to be scamming others online as well so either buy in store or avoid their online store.
