review_text: I bought an item through the online website with Paypal. I was not happy with their current quality and cut of the clothing. So I returned the item in-store within a week. But instead of getting my money back, I was given a giftcard. The lady said usually they transfer back the money, but she cant undo the giftcard. She supposedly called the customer service and they cant do anything. Now Im stuck with a Ǩ190 giftcard. I feel like Ive been robbed! Worst experience ever! Im going to sell my giftcard. Check ebay for my listing. Never buying there again!

review_title: Fabric used to be good, now awful and disposable
review_text: I used to love Zara. While I still love the styles, virtually everything in the store is now made of viscose, lyocell and rayon, meaning its good for one wear and then it looks like garbage and is fit for the trash. When I walk into the store and see all the cool clothes made out of disposable fabric, it makes me sick. What is the point of making all these clothes for just one good wear? Its a terrible, terrible waste. Step it up Zara. Ive since decided to spend more money on quality fabrics that will not only last, but will look better with time - like a good cashmere.

review_title: I bought this really nice coat for myĶ
review_text: I bought this really nice coat for my girlfriend and she loves it. Quality wise is good too, but that's not the thing I want to talk about. Their customer service is something that needs great improvement! I was in their store 2 weeks ago looking for the black coat that my girlfriend wants and I asked for help in looking for it. I had to wait almost 30 minutes just standing by a clothes rack. Zara staff were just walking by me and no one even bothered to ask if I needed any help. The shop seems understaffed too, with 1 cashier counter open and a line of customers waiting to buy their clothes.

review_title: Order not received. Terrible Customer Service
review_text: Order number [REDACTED_PHONE]. Order of around ¬£70 worth of items online. Hermes state that I received and signed for them, at home... While I'm at work 15 miles away. I haven't received anything. After waiting 2 days for Zara to "investigate" I was told "The courier has stated this was delivered", case closed. I already know this, I WAS IN WORK!!!! About 20 people and CCTV can attest to this. 6 Days later and no communication from Customer Service despite my messages to them every day, and no refund. Breaking Consumer Rights Law and also Consumer Contracts Regulations by doing so. - EDIT - Finally received my refund 6 days later after quoting Consumer Law and email/harassing every day.

review_title: I ordered my Zara package 5th MarchĶ
review_text: I ordered my Zara package 5th March 2019 and I am still waiting for it to arrive. Hermes claimed to attempt to deliver on 4 occasions, however this is untrue. Terrible service from both Zara and Hermes.

Look at the Euro/sterling price change!! Jumper priced at 29.95 Euros, selling in UK for ¬£39.99. Customer service very poor--could not justify these prices, unwilling to help, and defensive. Have spent a lot of money with Zara in the past--not now!!
Fabric has gone so cheap! Quality used to be great :(

Rude till personnel. Think it was actually the manager. She asked the customers in queue who were buying and said i am but she said, you are next anyway in a very sarcastic tone. Because of this will return the items i bought dont think i would like to support company who have rude staff.

review_title: Good variety but quality very low.
review_text: Good variety but quality very low. Once I go to the store, I obviously want not one-time clothes. Doesn't worth the price.

Will never shop here again! Bought a pair of new season shoes for my daughter as a gift. They were too small so returned to store unworn, still with the tags attached and the the receipt. Asked to exchange them for a different size, but was told that the receipt date had expired (by 5 days) so they couldn't be exchanged. I advised I didn't want a refund, simply an exchange for the same item in a different size was told they would not accept them back but I could purchase a new pair in a different size!! I laughed and said do you realise how ridiculous that sounds, and the manager replied, thats the policy.

review_title: Bullying, humiliation and intimidation by Management
review_text: Bullying, humiliation and intimidation is the customer service that is promoted within your meadowhall store by Zara Management. There was no duty of care displayed from any of the staff. After trying to return a faulty item and speaking to Zara head office, receiving a case reference number for a full refund, the manager told us we were lying as head office would never do that, she called head office behind the till and came back saying the case reference wasnt for a full refund. I called head office from my personal phone, gave the number to be told it says full refund, I told them the manager stated it didnt and they asked me to pass the phone over to her, she refused to take the call. The managers manner and behaviour was very intimidating, speaking down to us with great disrespect. Id like to remind the manager, not everyone works at Zara therefore you need to take some time to explain your policies and procedures, not try and show off in front of your friends at the till and call customers liars. This went on for about 15 minutes, the manager was very dismissive once I stated the consumer rights act, and kept asking other staff members to serve us without a solution. We were put in a very vulnerable position, my mum left the store in tears and hasnt returned to meadowhall since this incident as she is extremely intimidated, threatened and anxious of how the manager tried to personally embarrass us in front of all the staff at the tills and about 15 customers queueing. A customer was put in a terrible and unprofessional situation due to the incompetence of the Zara management and Zara head office. Looking at the below reviews, the management at Zara represent a very unprofessional culture. I am not satisfied with this service or how it has been dealt with so far. I would like this escalating and a respond immediately.

First time buyer Purchased dress online for first time. Dress was meant to be delivered on 3 days later. It's been a week and dress is still not here. I checked status of order today and the notes still states "preparing order". Customer service is appalling and severe delays with delivery. I wouldn't purchase from anything online from Zara.
Terrible customer service. Store processes are inadequately set up and staff on the phone are unhelpful. As a customer I have spent thousands over the last few years so would expect better!

review_title: Beware worse online shopping experience EVER
review_text: placed my first order of two items from Zara online , returned both for refund as unsuitable , both items returned within 3 days of receiving in original packaging & unworn . Had email stating my refund was being processed but only 1 item was mention , called to ask about other item to be told that they would not refund this item as it had been returned in used condition, this was absolutely untrue as the item was a pair of boots that I had tried on in my bedroom & had not even walked around room in them as they were to tight , also the boots where tied together with elastic cord which I hadnt even undone . But they basically said I was lying and would not listen to my side , this was my first and last order with Zara absolutely disgusted how can they get away with this sort of thing , feel like Ive been defrauded, I have been online shopping with most of the high street stores over the last 10yrs and can honestly say I have never experienced anything like this before .Only wish I had looked at the reviews on trustpilot before I ordered .

review_title: If I could give a zero I would
review_text: If I could give a zero I would. Horrible customer service when returning an unworn item that I changed my mind on. They claim that is smells like perfume so they cannot return. It was literally tried on and then put back in the box to return. Snotty customer service reps and a nasty email from the company it just the end of it all. This makes me question ever shopping at Zara again. Beware of buying and returning at Zara. They will fraudulently say it is unsaleable so they don't have to take it back.

Please please do not shop here unless you want a shock when you want to return an item, go somewhere like new look or topman/topshop instead or maybe river island but dont go to Zara

review_title: If i could give Zara 0 stars i would!!!!
review_text: Returned two items, one was terrible quality, probably wore it for an hour and the lining was shedding everywhere. The other item i had on my body for barely a second, it was too small. Both were sent back to me with no explanation. I rung up to ask what was going on, they opened a dispute and said they would contact me within 24-48 hours. They had no record of the item that was too small, definitely just chucked it in with the other. They never contacted me, i rung up after 3 days and they said that nothings been done and i should wait until they contact me. A week later i ring up as they obviously couldn't be bothered to contact me, they said nope no refund as both items have been worn. One was bad quality/faulty and one wasn't touched!!! Shocking service I've never experienced this before, they have wrongfully taken my money from me and they couldn't care less. Will never spend a penny in Zara again. You would do yourself a favour by avoiding them at all costs. Wish i had seen all these bad reviews before i ordered from them Edit - I went into store and returned the item that was too small, they didn't ask any questions and processed the refund in under a minute. Shows how wrong and unreasonable the factory/customer service teams are

review_title: I am normally a lover of Zara products but...
review_text: I am normally a lover of Zara products however I recently ordered from them for it to be delivered to a collection point. My husband who was standing to the side said he was shocked by the service I received ( or didnt). I usually shop at Milton Keynes store and have never received bad treatment there I may add.

review_title: never ever have I been treated so BADLYĶ

I ENDED UP WITH NO REFUND AND HOUR and HLAF OF WASTED TIME> NEVER EVER Buying with zara and supporting them.

review_title: Very Very Very Poor Customer Service
review_text: Never before have I received such poor customer service. I returned a top JANUARY 2019 which, having washed it only once, had multiple holes appear in it. Clearly cheaply sourced and cheaply made. The online chats were a complete waste of time - the language barriers from an outsourced call centre very poor (spoke with Arief, Saiqa and Palio, etc. Shaun, Customer Service Manager in Zara Customer Services, refused to refund me. Having spoke to their Head Office, it is clear they will do everything they can to get out of refunding the customer, I emailed the CEO, Ana Oliveira, in January, February, March and she didnt bother to reply to a single email, shocking! Head Office state that the top wasnt returned. I have PROOF, Royal Mail proof of sending, a receipt, photographs of the top, photos of the addressed envelope, etc. but still they claim they have no responsibility to refund me. As a result, they have lost three customers in my household, we will no longer shop with them. Atrocious lack of service in this day and age. review_text: But the employees really need to stop being so rude to customers. They lack proper training on how to deal with customer complaints and just end up escalating most complaints to unecessary levels.

review_title: Had to wait 11 days for an order

review_title: I bought my grandaughter an outfit andĶ

Visited Canterbury store fitting room assistant rude! Felt like we were being rushed when trying on our clothes. Not happy did not say thank you at till after we made a purchase. More fitting rooms needed as clearly not enough!

Shocking customer service .. I returned an item only to be told they would not refund me as the item had a powdery smell to it ... after asking for an explanation of the powdery smell I was told that I had worn the item and washed it and returned it .... after asking for an explanation on how I had worn the item and washed it and how the paper label was still intact I was told that I had removed the item as it was attached with a pin .... the label is not attached with a pin its a label on a plastic thread that goes into the dress .... I asked to speak to head office to complain ... I got a further phone call from the same guy not head office .... no refund ....Zara customer service basically will make anything up and lie if they dont want to refund you ... be warned their customer service is appalling... Id give zero stars if I could ...... shocking

review_title: I ordered some items online on ThursdayĶ
review_text: I ordered some items online on Thursday and selected the Next Day Delivery service - in which the order should arrive on Saturday due to the bank holiday weekend. I received an email which gives you an option to select the day/evening slot in which I selected the day slot (10am - 5pm). I waited all day for my package with no sign of its whereabouts (the tracker is absolutely useless as it doesnt show any information of where/when you are likely to receive - just a map of your current location). I gave them the benefit of the doubt due to it being a Saturday and maybe busy and was hoping to at least receive it later that evening - it was 8pm by now so I contacted the customer service via the chat option online. The lady assisted to check but informed me that there is a delay and that I am likely to receive this in 2 days time in which I mentioned that I paid for Next day delivery and also received a text that morning stating that I would receive my delivery that very day. Instead of helping to make the situation right as customer services are supposed to do, the lady just said that they have taken my concerns to heart and will try their best to do better next time. Sorry but this is all talk and no action! I am left in the position wondering where and when my package will arrive and really annoyed by the fact that I specifically selected the next day service as I would be at home to receive it. Not good enough!

review_title: The worst dealing with the customers...
review_text: The worst dealing with the customers... I hate the brand the dealing the way that operator assistant was attending the complaint's.. Zara a big no to u from future...

I contacted customer service straight away to report the problem with images they said they was going to look into it & come back to me. When they did come back to me they told me they are unable to send out a replacement as they have no more left at all. When I asked them if i kept this dress what could be done I was told that this would need to be reviewed & they would come back to me. when they did come back they offered me free delivery on my next order! I cant believe this is all they were offering the item i already had arrived in such bad condition & they offered me free delivery on my next order! I told them this would not be needed as I would not be ordering from ZaraOnline Again!!

review_title: Poor quality products contribute to landfill
review_text: Zara clothes look great...until you wash them, then they are finished. Here's what happened: bought an embroidered dress, 2 pairs of cords and.a shirt, i carefully handwashed them all after wearing and now; the shirt is threadbare and bobbly, the cords are bobbly, one pair has developed a streak in the colour, a result of a poor dyeing process, the dress has discoloured in several places and has a black streak from dye transfer from the.embroidery. By sourcing poor fabrics, the garments have a short life and will quickly go to.landfill. Is this really what you want Zara? To contribute to the death of our planet? By contrast, my H&M and Topshop items are standing the test of time. Until Zara ups the quality so that clothes last longer, I will not be.shopping here again.

On 8th April I received confirmation that my order was ready for collection. On collection day the shop assistant handed me someone else's order. He checked again for my order and informed me that order isn't there and that customer service will be in touch with me regarding this issue. After several days there was no communication I have sent several emails enquiring about my order and haven't received any reply. On 15th of April I rang to customer service desks and was advised that they will be intouch with me in 48hrs, no reply since then.

review_title: What a load of rubbish bought lovelyĶ

I only ticked gift receipt as some of the items were to be a gift. Unfortunately the items I returned which were all for me I now have 60 of online credit for a store I rarely shop in. Lesson learnt! Never tick gift receipt & I personally wont ever be shopping online here again (once I have tried to find a way to spend 60which I have more need of in my bank account).

review_text: Would never order online or in the shop again !!!!! Shocking!!!!

review_title: Do not shop online unless you're happy waiting 2 weeks for a Next Day delivery service.
review_text: I've loved Zara since I was 16 and most of my wardrobe comes from Zara however over the last 2 years they have gone downhill and I've had to complain about the customer service in store on multiple occasions after queuing for 40 minutes and not even getting a simple "sorry" from the checkout girls. The quality isn't always fantastic but you need to use your own judgement with the price as some ¬£30.00 have lasted me years, whereas the cheaper items from their basic range perhaps last just 1 summer. Last year I shopped online at Zara quite frequently and every single time I had an issue. I bought some holiday clothes after my store ran out of my sizes, I paid for next day delivery as I was due to go on holiday 3 days after I bought my items. Needless to say they never showed up, and when I returned they still weren't delivered - I finally got my items after 2 weeks and wasn't offered a refund on my next day delivery. After this I vowed never to shop online with them again, but I couldn't resist the boxing days sales. I bought about 8 items which were split into 2 shipments, the first arrived within a few days as I got them click and collect to my local postoffice which was great, I was then waiting another week for the second which I'd come to understand is quite standard Zara delivery practice. I needed to return half of the items so opted for a collection from home, I booked it online and waited for the driver to come a few days later - no show. SO I contacted the customer service who apologised and booked it again for the following day- no show. I contacted them AGAIN and the customer service agent told me that no collections have ever been booked (despite booking two) - Anyway... the items were finally collected 10 days after I tried to return them which was frustrating as you have to be in for the collections. I was refunded pretty quickly, which is the only prompt thing that Zara can offer.

They sent me a bag which had ALDO engraved on the zip tag. After contacting customer service they responded and asked if i want a replacement or refund to which i asked fo a refund. Was told this was sent to their finance department and will be in my account in 24-48 hours. Of course nothing in there 3 days later... i contacted them to then be told that i had to return the item in order to get my refund. Unfortunately i gave the bag to charity as i trusted this issue was solved. They then refused to give me a refund even though they clearly understood i was promised one initially by the agent i first spoke to. Absolutely disgusting customer service. Ill never buy from them again. Rubbish manufacturing and customer service. Maybe i should send this to aldo and see how they feel about zara selling goods with their logo on it.
Terrible customer service no assistance if questions asked they answer as they do you favor. Been to 2 stores in Dubai. Never Zara again

Yes you can only take 6 in, the rest you have to bring back into the shop. Or you can put them here but in a second, employees will take the cloths again to bring them back in the shop. So I literally had to go 2 extra times to make a round in the shop to find the items what I wanted to fit, had to put 3 times my cloths on and off because I had to find the other pieces in the shop again and had to pay 3 times... I fel this was SO unfriendly for a customer and SO time consuming....

review_title: I went into the store in Bolton LancsĶ
review_text: I went into the store in Bolton Lancs on 16/06/2019 at 13.37 for a refund. I was polite to the assistant who was serving at the till. His attitude was quite cold and did not even hint at any pleasantness. I could have tolerated this but then when he gave me my refund receipt he didnt even look at me and said nothing. I was left wondering if the transaction was completed. I asked him if that was it and he just said yes. I was left feeling totally uncomfortable and dissatisfied with the experience. I shop there regularly but I wont go into that store again. I went back into complain a couple of minutes later and asked him for the store Manager snd again he said nothing and pointed to her. There is no name of the assistant on the receipt and he did not wear a badge with anything of any kind. There are so many numbers on the receipt I dont know which one proves my visit. The transaction number at the top of the receipt is 74569. I have worked in retail management for a long time and I am finding more and more of this poor service which in this climate is unacceptable.
Unbelievably rude staff. Wont shop here ever again. But Im sure the staff couldnt care less.

review_text: Zara are stating they delivered a package to my home, however no package has been delivered. I advised Zara that I have not received this and have since had the worst customer service I have ever received. This has been ongoing nine days and someone has come back to me to say the query is being closed down and that my claim wouldnt be considered. Ive asked for this to be escalated further to management and they have said this cant be done. I have found the entire customer services process absolutely disguising. I will never order from here again. 60 down and useless staff.

The total amount of the original gift was 19,99, so I chose what I wanted and went to the cashier who said that she couldnt finish the transaction because the things that I chose was 19,98. Yes, because of 0,01 I would have to buy something else which I didnt want to! If she said either she could not give me any money back or any credit, would be my choice to buy something else and then pay for the difference or just leave it, but because of 0,01 I certainly wouldnt bother. To make worse, the cashier pretended she wasnt seeing me when I came back to the till after choose a T-shirt to finish the transaction. My baby was crying and I just wanted to get my stuffs and go away. For me, this is just a way to force people to spend money in their stores which is not very intelligent once I had a very negative experience and I will never go back again.

review_title: Horrendous experience in my lunch hour- avoid when Sales on
review_text: Horrendous experience in my lunch hour Bromley branch has no contingency in Plac on what to do at sale time or in peak hours of the day like lunch time. Way too many people on the shop floor with only one cashier on at a double cashpoint desk- why? Surely you must have worked out that all the cash desk need to be manned, may be one needs to be for returns only? Why so many returns - well because the changing room queue is so long - people are buying in bulk, taking the stuff home to try on at home only to discover it doesnt fit as your sizes are the most generous and then they hope to pop back in for a refund only to find themselves in a queue for 30mins. You must realise that you are missing out on sales surely - people have time limits in their lunch hour - move with the times - be proactive - stop for a minute and put yourself in the shoes of a customer - oh yes - you get it now?! Great may be could now apply some logic and find some staff with managerial skills - it shouldnt take a customer in a queue buying 1 item to flag to a staff member that the queue is growing, there is only one cashier on and we are now all on borrowed time!!!

review_title: I just had the worst customerĶ
review_text: I just had the worst customer experience trying to return a defective item which I purchased from Zara. They were only going to give me a refund at half price because the item I bought was now on sale but I refused. I was made to spend 45mins on the phone with a customer service rep trying to trace the transaction via my credit card details before I would be refunded my money because I didn't have the receipt any more. It is unfair and unreasonable for these stores to expect that customers would still have receipts of purchases 3 months later. If an item is poor quality, customers shouldn't have to jump hoops to get a refund. Absolutely ridiculous, thanks Zara for wasting an hour of my life I'll never get back. At least I got my money in the end even if i left feeling frustrated. My advice to anyone who faces a similar problem and doesn't have their receipt is to get on the phone with customer service and insist they trace the transaction via your credit card details and the store bought from but you'll have to do this in store so they can speak with the manager and confirm the details for the store to process the refund. You can also do this in any Zara store.

review_title: How is this company even in business
review_text: How is this company even in business! Everything I read about them talks of unethical behavior and shady practices! HORRIBLE experience. Please read and share. Have gone round and round with customer service and they've advise me that I checked a box to receive a "gift receipt" when I checked out online. Step 4 states: "Refund - The refund will in the same method of payment as your order. Please allow 10-14 days for a full refund of your returned articles." DO NOT. I REPEAT DO NOT GIVE THIS COMPANY ANY OF YOUR BUSINESS!

review_title: One Big Mess of A Fashion Brand
review_text: 1st time shopping at Zara I went in-store at 1 of the Oxford Street (London) branches. Staff were ok but wouldn't say they were the friendliest. My mum wasn't allowed to come into the changing rooms with me despite the fact I'm a minor. The stock/range they retail in-store is very limited compared to online, and couldn't really find any of the cool trendy items from their collections. Shopping online, my experience was a lot better. I was fully satisfied with the service I received, I was regularly updated regarding tracking info, and items arrived very quickly. I needed to return an item, and the whole process was simple and easy, and I was refunded in a reasonable time period. My only real complaint about Zara is their sizing and fit. Unlike Inditex's other subsidiaries, Zara clothes are designed and made for the classic European body type, much like the models you see on their website. In other words, the clothes are made for tall and skinny women. I bought their "Premium Skinny Flare Jeans". I wear a UK 6, and at the time I had a 24" waist and 34.5" hips, and size 6 fit me perfectly - I've never had a pair of jeans which fit me both in the waist and hips. I also have a bigger/protruding bum and it fitted over it comfortably. The only problem was the length was WAY too long as I'm 5'3 and have a 29" leg inseam. Zara had not made it clear that their trousers/jeans have long inseams. I returned these and bought their "Mini Flares" in same size instead, which ironically did not fit me like the 1st pair. Despite fitting my leg inseam better as they are 'cropped' (were full length on me), they were too big in the waist, tight in hips and thighs, and as they were mid-rise could not get up over my bum. Also bought a pair of mom shorts in size 6, and they were really big on me even for mom shorts.... May I please also note that my sister bought a pair of mom jeans which fitted her perfectly, and she has a traditional pear-shaped figure with wide hips. She's also short like me (5'4) and the leg inseam of these jeans fitted her... Seems to me that Zara's sizing is confused and inconsistent, and that's what makes them a hit-or-miss retailer. For how rich the company are, they should be at least selling their jeans in different leg inseams - we can't all be tall! They also should be more mindful of different body shapes and silhouettes when producing clothes. Quality of clothes aren't great, my 1st pair of jeans were ok, but my 2nd pair of jeans and shorts feel cheaply made. Pull & Bear and Bershka products are better quality from my personal experience. Overall Zara do sell some really cute, trendy and slightly bougie products. However in-store range is rubbish, and beware before you buy that their clothes are made for figures similar to their models.... :3

Really poor after service. I have waited 3 weeks for a refund from an item bought online and each time I have been Fobbed off with one excuse after an other. No one calls you back & the live chat is useless...you may as well be speaking to a robot.

I told him multiple times alright if it is in the policy to buy them I will and 5 minutes later I will walk back to the store and return them(which is fully my right) he kept saying no and reminded us the 2 options mentioned above. When asked to call the manager he said that he is the manager which I dont believe it at all. Local people tried to approach us to offer help and he talked to them in dutch( I have no idea what he said but it seemed rude). At the end he asked us if were scared and my sister said no but we just dont want to loose time. Honestly I fully understand the situation that it can seem that we tried to steal a pair of jeans but I just think that a thief wouldnt go back to the racks to return it but he/she would be running out of the store. This is the worst way to treat costumers by making them believe they are wrong and scaring them if they call the police. The store has cameras and they had the chance to see everything that went on, but all they cared was to get our money. If you want sales make your product sell not our fear. This is the worst costumer service!!!!!

review_title: Disgusting service and policies blaming customers
review_text: How can you give ZERO stars?! Loathe this company. Aside from the fact the quality of the clothing has been on a downward spiral for a while, I purchased a dress on a Saturday with the intention of wearing it the Sunday. I paid for the premium same-day delivery service in London. It arrived, and as I usually do, I put my clothes through a quick rinse when new before I wear. The dress came out blotchy and ruined - the dye had run everywhere, it was a mess. Bear in mind the dress had been in my possession all of 4 hours and I followed washing temps etc, using the same detergent I use to wash babies clothes in. Tried to find an email address to contact, none. Used the online chat system, told them what had happened and was told to contact via Whatsapp. Text via Whatsapp with images and told someone would get back to me. A week later get an email saying they had been trying to call and I needed to call them back (BTW NO missed calls at all, lies). Called them up and told the design team said I used bleach in the wash. WHAT?! 1. Why would I use bleach in my washing machine to wash clothes?! 2. Were your design team present when I washed my clothes?! So how could they know I used bleach?! Unacceptable accusations they are levying at their paying customers which I refused to accept. Also had to educate girl on the phone regarding their return policy as she thought it was only 30 days to return any item, when in fact an item can be returned after 30 days if it is faulty. Worrying right there that they cannot even train their staff on their policies for a start and I had to read it out to her. Not that it mattered, I reported this dress as faulty 4 hours after receipt! Was told someone would get back to me, didn't hear a thing. Decided I don't want to give this vile company my money so I returned everything else I bought from them recently, including another faulty dress (again, unworn, collar totally unravelled in first the wash, shock) and I sent the blotchy dress in question back. Another week later, received an email to again say they tried to call me but no response (LIES, NO missed calls...or emails, or WhatsApp messages or anything) simply say my dispute has been rejected and they will be posting the blotchy dress back to me. So essentially, rather than accept fault of the product, they assume their paying customers are idiotic liars who use bleach to wash their clothes; so much so they actually go out of their way to return their faulty items to said customers? Wow, I guess that's a way to ensure you keep your profits - selling shi*ty products and then refusing to give refunds. Have had equally bad experiences with staff in their Croydon store who seem to act like asking for a refund is coming from their own personal pocket, not money customers have already spent and are within their rights to get back in a return. Suffice to say, whatever parcel they return to me will be rejected. A horrible toxic company, thank God I don't work in a place like that and will never ever shop there again; I'd rather thrown ¬£50's into the Thames than give them another penny of my money. Conglomerate bullies.

review_title: Buyer Beware: Zara will steal from you.
review_text: This happened five or six months ago, but I'm still livid about Zara's theft & incompetence, so I'm going to share my experience.¬† If nothing else, I can warn others so they can avoid bitter experiences.¬† I placed an order (#[REDACTED_PHONE]), of which I kept some items and returned the rest for varying reasons.¬† When I received the return acceptance e-mail (return #93572798) some time later, I noticed that it was partial.¬† ¬†None of the five t-shirts I sent back were included in the e-mail or refund amount. Puzzled, I rang Zara's customer service line.¬† I explained that the t-shirts were missing and asked that they correct it, adding that the odds of a mistake on my end were ZERO - I packed everything myself, was supremely methodical, triple-counted, then sealing and delivered it myself.¬† I was told it would be investigated, which was fine by me.¬† I told them that the fact that JUST THE T-SHIRTS were missing was probably a clue that they may have been sorted by category, or set aside for some reason. On March 20th, I got a voicemail from a "Sandrine", giving me case number¬†35699932.¬† ¬†I rang back and was told that regrettably, they couldn't assist me.¬† I said, very politely, that this news was unacceptable.¬† I spent time repeating everything.¬† I told her that she could not expect me to tell her what's wrong with THEIR operation - I have no way of knowing if it was theft from one of their employees, if the t-shirts had been misplaced by their staffers, if their supply chain systems were broken, or what in the world had happened.¬† It was their responsibility to find out, not mine.¬† I had done my part and sent the items.¬† The person said that my issue would be escalated further, and I thanked her.¬† ¬†Surely they would investigate properly and I would get good news, right? Well, guess again.¬† ¬†On March 23rd, I got a voicemail from a "Raquel" calling from¬†[REDACTED_PHONE].¬† She referenced a new case number:¬†35858550.¬† More stonewalling, their "investigation" had turned up nothing.¬† I said that I failed to see how that was my problem.¬† What, exactly, did this so-called "investigation" entail?¬† Didn't they have cameras in their warehouses to check such things?¬† ¬†How was it my responsibility to tell her what had gone wrong on their end? The onus was on Zara to figure out why and how they misplaced five t-shirts I sent back (packed with the items they did refund).¬† How are any of us otherwise supposed to prove what we sent back?!?¬† Even if I had taken photos of everything packed neatly on its way back, that proves nothing.¬† And it frankly feels offensive - not only are they implying that I'm either a thief or didn't send what I know I packed, but they're keeping my money too!¬† It's beyond the pale. The person I spoke to suggested that, to avoid this, returns can be done in store too.¬† I told her that I'm not going to drive an hour and a half into the closest Zara store, with the fuel and parking fees and toll fees that entails, just to avoid their incompetence with online returns.¬†¬† Four of the returned t-shirts that Zara lost/misplaced/miscounted were $17.90 each and the fifth was $9.90.¬† That's $81.50...let's say about $88 with tax.¬† Am I going to sue Zara for $88-ish bucks?¬† Of course not, I'm not going to cut off my nose to spite my face, and end up wasting more time and money than this is worth.¬† And past a certain point of spending hours on the phone, with zero assistance from Zara's people, I'm not going to spend days arguing pointlessly til I'm blue in the face.¬† ¬†After a certain point, my time is worth more.¬†¬† I was left with no real solution except to let Zara steal from me.¬† And make no mistake - it's theft, it's fraud, and it's outrageous.¬† Whether it happened because of bad business processes, employee theft, warehouse incompetence, or miscommunication doesn't change that fact (and is not my problem).¬† THIS COULD BE YOU TOO - Zara is not accountable to its customers, no matter what their policies say.¬†¬†¬† I cannot force Zara to pay what they rightfully owe me, and it's a relatively small amount.¬† You know when you feel like you've been taken advantage of and are powerless to change it?¬† ¬†It stings, right?¬† The principle bothers me a lot more than the amount involved.¬† This happened months ago, but now I've decided to do something about it. I believe in accountability.¬† So here's what I'm doing to hold Zara responsible for failing to refund the rest of the order I sent, hiding behind their supposed "investigation": 1.¬† I resolved to never, ever buy a single thing from Zara again, unless this is repaid to me (which it won't be).¬† I've been purchasing their clothing all over the world for 20 years now.¬† No more.¬† I've likewise advised my friends & family to avoid them as well.¬†¬†I mean, would YOU trust them again?¬† There are far too many great clothing brands to go back to someone who stole your money when they're at fault.¬† I now buy from Bonobos (which costs a bit more, but have great discounts), Club Monaco, and on the cheaper end, Uniqlo and H&M.¬†¬† Zara may have kept my $88, but over the long-term, they will lose thousands of dollars in profit from me and my family because of their mistake.¬† Being a giant conglomerate, they won't care, but I care that I'm holding them accountable.¬† Long term, I win and Zara loses. 2.¬† I'm going to disseminate my story everywhere I can: Yelp, Resolver, the Better Business Bureau, the FTC, Google reviews, and on and on.¬† If you steal my money, you better believe I'm going to spread the word.¬†¬† So long, Zara.¬† You got the better of me, but I'm going to hold you responsible before I forget you.

Placed an order on 9/7, received email on 9/8 that merchandise was on its way. Never received package even though it was indicated that it was delivered on 9/9. Contacted Zara via tracking page and filled out inquiry regarding missing package. Noted that it was sent to the incorrect address. Received call back from CSR that this issue was being investigated and I should hear back in 2-3 business days. In the meantime, I should check with my neighbors on the block to see if they have my package. Received a call back on 9/18, stating that per the investigation, merchandise was sent and received 9/9 to the address and picture was taken when left in door step. I explained that the number that was listed does not exist. How and who did they leave it with? She said stated they have a picture and I requested to see the picture because I did not receive anything and wanted to see where it was delivered. As I waited for 10 minutes, she said they were having technical difficulties and I would have to wait 24 hours. So, no merchandise, no replacement and charged for nothing. Then told again to go to surrounding neighborhood houses to reclaim my product!! Excuse me?? I will NEVER shop or order a product from your unprofessional, crappy quality store! I want my money back and hope to find the product so that I can obtain! Never have I had this kind of customer experience! Your corporation should invest in training and improving your protocols/policies. I will make sure to continue posting this experience so that it deters customers from your store.

Order: [REDACTED_PHONE] My first time experience with Zara has been a complete joke. My order was said to have come through and signed for "by me" yesterday at 12:31pm. But that was ridiculous, I was in my toilet and would have certainly have heard the bell since I left the door open for my order. It did not make sense. After a couple of hours touring every bloody door on my street for my stuff, I now believe that these people are a bunch of liars and thieves. There was no package delivered what so ever. Whats worse is, I saw a courier at that particular time and he said that he had nothing for me. I know some other people have had to deal with this same issue and my advice would be to press charges under section 75 of the Consumer Act which covers orders from ¬£100 - ¬£30k of value. At least the very threat of it would make them reconsider a refund or resending the order with a more competent courier. The customer service itself has been very apologetic but have really done nothing. I've been waiting for a basic response, such as to which house my package has been sent to. None. I will never be ordering from Zara again, best bet will be to physically go to their stores and purchase whatever from there.

Appalling customer service. Parcel was left at my front door without my permission, and 6 weeks later it is clear it has gone missing. Although courier company cannot provide any evidence it was even delivered (Hermes), apparently this is my fault/responsibility and I am not entitled to a refund. This means I am ¬£120 out of pocket for something which is not my fault! I am utterly disgusted by this, and even more so by all the chasing and ineptitude of the customer service team to deal with such a problem.

Shocking customer service. I ordered two tops from Zara one was too big the other had a dirty mark on it. I jumped through hoops to return the items (they no longer provide a return label which is fine unless you dont have a printer) and went online to fill in the return form. There were tick box options so I ticked faulty for the dirty top as there was no dirty option. Around a week later I got an email to say they had processed my refund, but when I checked my account they had only refunded the cost of one of the tops. I phoned customer services to query this and they said they didnt refund one of the items as Id returned it dirty. I explained that there was no tick box for dirty so I ticked faulty. The customer service representative said they would look into it and contact me within a week to let me know if they would refund me. In the meantime I ordered a briefcase from Zara as a gift for my husband. I got a text from Hermes saying that the parcel had been delivered but I hadnt recieved it despite being in all day. I called Zara again to explain the situation and to request they send another briefcase out and they said that they could not help until an investigation had been made into the missing briefcase. They said that as I hadnt ticked the box requesting a signature on delivery that the parcel was therefore my responsibility from the moment it left their warehouse and if it was lost they would not be liable. At this point I am 85 down with no products to show for it and Zara were doing everything in their power to avoid taking any responsibility whatsoever. I asked to speak to a manager and was told they dont have any managers ??? and was told Id have to wait up to a week for a call back. I eventually received a refund for the top and luckily for me the Hermes driver came later to deliver the briefcase. Zara never once called me back about either issue. I will never shop with Zara again after the way I was treated.

review_title: Zara don't care about their customer
review_text: Zara doesn't know how to give the extra mile to their loyal customers. No matter how much you supported this brand, how much money you spent or how long you have been a customer when you made one small mistake, Zara is not there to help you. I accidentally purchased wrong size shoes from Zara store outside my country, you see, I will not be able to return this item until I go back to that country. I contacted Zara to ask if I can return the item in my country or even allow me to have an extended return policy since the only option is to send the item back to the country where I bought the shoes. Zara just said NO to all options and stuck to their policy. I know policies are there for a reason but they should still have some consideration according to the circumstances. Unfortunately for Zara, I've has similar issue with companies like Triangl and The White Company, both companies allowed me to have extended return policy because they want to help me and they want me to be happy. Unfortunately, Zara is nowhere near these two companies when it comes to customer service. Zara deserves to fail!

review_title: Shockingly bad-in my case ZARA robbed me in daylight!
review_text: First of all I would not even alocate 1 star. The company fails in their online sales activity in every sense of it. I have now been trying to resolve my non delivered order for which i paid, for 3 weeks now and moving to nowhere, so will be putting claim through court as the help and my voluntary cooperation takes me nowhere. They claim they have delivered my order and have signature - which they don't. I have been at home on that day that they claim they have delivered and there was even no attemp to do so. They claim that they are not going to refund since they have signature for delivery and when i asked to send me the copy of signature they send me a form with my adress but no signature!? Naturally because I didn't sign, even if they had any scrible that would be evident that it wasn't mine as again there was not even an attempt from dhl to deliver to my address, and i have a proof, as we have RING recording activity. I sent them all the recordings of that day - yet they still refuse to refund, there is no conversation about sending the goods as they are out of stock now. The phone officers just read out standard lines which do not offer any help, not sure why they employ humans as they are not being used beyond repeating standard lines - computer can do that. They refuse to put me to a manager, some kind of joke - i mean shocking for the company they put themselves to. I am a customer with physical evidence that there was no delivery and they are simply ignoring it - in my eyes this is a robbery. They also never respond to anything, no updates on the case, nothing, it's like communication is only one way, and it is from you only. I should not tell how frustrating it is as it is beyond any normal sense but even if you been getting your orders ok, know that if one fails you will be left without your money and any support from them.

I ordered 4 items online and as 2 didnt fit I attempted to return them. The instructions on the site when i logged into my account simply said to return for free to any royal mail collection point. The package was bulky and I had to get a bus and when I arrived they said they need a returns label. When I called zara they said i hadn't booked a return (which luckily i had done 2 days ago) but they failed to email me a return label. The lady said it can take up to an hour but I had waited 2 days already. The lady then told me to wait another hour. By which point I was very dissatisfied as I only had half hour left for lunch. The lady was very rude and when I tried to explain that I hadn't had any further instructions to email or online about return process she refused to accept that and said she never had this problem with any customer before. When I asked why the return label isn't displayed online when you booked a return she wasnt able to explain. I will never order online from Zara again.

review_title: Ive been shopping at Zara for yearsĶ
review_text: Ive been shopping at Zara for years but wont shop there anymore. I purchased a blouse three weeks ago and was excited to wear it on a getaway trip with my husband. I discovered, however, that the security tag was not removed. I brought it back to the store but was told they could not remove without receipt. I called my bank who confirmed time and date of purchase and are resending a monthly statement. I spoke with a manager and Zara customer service that said without receipt there is no obligation to remove the security tag. Im not sure why Im being punished for their error. I have paid for the item in full. Very disappointing. I will take my business elsewhere.

review_title: Bad customer service, inflexible returns policy.
review_text: Buyer Beware. I purchased a jacket in store, which I stumbled across on travels. A week later the lining fell to pieces, this was an obvious stitching fault. What I didnt know is that while you can return your online purchases by mail, Zara will not do this for store bought items. They require you to return them to a store. My nearest store is a 2 hour 40 minute round trip from my home. They refused to be reasonable and allow normal postal returns, refused to allow me to post direct to the store, and have now stopped communicating with me. They have my money and have sold me shoddy goods. Clearly they dont give a hoot for any environmental impact created with this absurd policy. Ive shopped with Zara for years, alas Im unlikely to continue.

review_title: poor customer service , hard to reach
review_text: have ordered on the website , i alway purchase with Zara all the time for my kids , unfortunately it delivery to my address that i lived 6 years agao , delivery address not exist anymore on my address book and my account details, so weird so i called customer service to stop delivery which is they didn't do as the driver called me and ask where am i so i told him to return the item back to Zara and now it shows that it delivered and no refund , have tried to contact zara live chat but been waiting for 10 mins now and going to call but just want to say that it seems so hard to contact them and delivery company

Customer Service is horrific!! I ordered shoes online, the package came within two days of order, however instead of shoes i got a man jacket! how can you possibly mix up shoes for a jacket?! I rang the customer service the second I discovered this and was advised I will not receive my shoes (the shoes i actually paid for and ordered!!) until i return the item they sent me, not only that but until they receive this! I tried to explain to the customer service that this is ridiculous and not my fault or problem for that matter that they sent me the wrong item! That I am more then happy to return the wrong item but they need to send me my shoes asap! They blankly refused! I posted the jacket first thing the next morning (as delivery I received was at 5pm) and rang the customer service AGAIN to say I posted the item I received and now want my shoes, to which I was advised it will take 24 hours to process 'my request' (as if they didn't ef up and it's my fault) and once the order has been processed the delivery will take up to two days. absolutely ridiculous service! zero care for customers! or any acknowledgments of their own mistakes!!

Bought Textured Weave Jumpsuit in Sept 2019. Nice item except it had manufacturing flaw on left shoulder that was pointed out to me when I first wore it. I tried to exchange it. Good luck. The customer service is the worst of any store I have shopped in. Chat, phone all bad. I just want a return now, and will never shop there again. These people are incompetent, don't care or both. So you get a garbage product and no customer service.

review_title: My jacket stole from ZARA and they wouldnt help!
review_text: Last Thursday I was shopping with my friend at ZARA Oxford street, and when I was trying on a new jacket, my old one was stolen from my baby buggy, I asked the staff if they picked it to the storage by considince, they were too lazy to help, and the next day I called ZARA costumer services to see if they can help or call the branch but they did nothing!! I come back home with no jacket and it was cold, I couldnt imagine this coud happen to my first and only leather jacket at one of the biggest brand like ZARA. So DISPPOINTED!!

review_title: ZARA 0 care 0 customer service. The worst retail customer service globally
review_text: ordered something over a week ago to arrive with next day delivery. The order never came. I have called, emailed and used the live check with ZARA several times to the same response. There is nothing we can do,its down to our courier. The order was quite expensive and still no sign of it and now the tacking number for my order on hermes does not even exist. ZARA has the worst customer service and ownership for taking responsibility for its orders. ZARA uses Hermes , The worst courier company in London to deliver their parcels, by choosing the courier they use some responsibility should be taken if customers have issues with getting their orders, as at the end of the day I am Shopping with ZARA not Hermes. I have always loved ZARA products but after this experience I wouldn't recommend shopping with them or even consider shopping with them again as I am out of pocket, without my order and was given no support or apology and all the customer service did was say you need to contact the courier but cannot provide an email address, phone number or any information for that matter on how to get my parcel . Horrendous service

By far, the worst customer service interaction I've had in the recent, competitive retail landscape. I ordered a pair of shoes over a week ago, and received a notification that a shipment label was created. The tracker link has been showing it as "shipment label created" for over a week. I reached out to a customer service rep via their online chat portal, and this person was extremely rude. First, the response is as follows to my question of where is my item? "I apologize for this delay in your order. This is not the experience we would want you to have. I will put this in for an internal review. We will work diligently to resolve this in a timely manner. Is there anything else I can help you with today?" OH, OK. Thanks for repeating facts to me without offering a solution. The person then says this has to go through a review which will take over 2 weeks. When I asked for the order to be cancelled and refunded since apparently they can't provide any information, the person responded "I'm very sorry, however, as this has is too far into the warehouse, we can't cancel the order" Look, Zara. You're not special. This is the era of instagram influencers, and online boutiques. Retail giants are filing chapter 11 bankruptcy left and right. The one thing you can do to ensure you have repeat customers you can retain is to provide valuable customer service. You have definitely lost my business as someone who has exclusively shopped Zara for a decade. HASTA LA VISTA! I'll stay tuned for your bankruptcy news as it looks like I'm not the only one with a terrible experience.

review_title: Zara have no morals or respect for their customers
review_text: Awful quality clothing. Most of it is shiny acrylic rubbish. Used to be good with returns, refund issued within a day or two of the return being received but theyve had a significant value return for over 2 weeks now and are still looking into it with their warehouse as to whats happened before I can have my money back. They have the proof of postage to show it was received by them but refuse to refund until it been investigated. Customer service, if you can call it that, are rude and make false promises about when youll be contacted back. In fact they have now told me I cannot have a timeframe for a resolution, itll just be when its been looked into and repeat x1000. Wont be buying from them again after this and I suggest you dont either. Off to ask for a chargeback via my card provider. Zara is a scummy disreputable company with no morals an no respect for their customers.
Very bad and slowly

review_title: Pathetic way to treat a customer
review_text: I recently purchased a coat and jeans from Zara online. Delivery was supposed to have been on the 3rd of October 2019 its the 9th of October 2019 and I still have not received my order. I have emailed, used their chat service, reached out on Twitter, but to no avail. I have been lied to saying the driver attempted delivery and I have proof to show that no delivery attempt was ever made. They use a delivery company called Paack who are equally useless. Zara have charged my card and now say they wont deliver after three failed delivery attempts which is a blatant lie. I have sat home for three days from 10:00-22:00 tracking the driver who messages saying driver on way only to then message exactly a minute later to say, sorry unable to deliver. The third day when I checked my tracking again the driver was in Northampton when I live in East London and Zara still wont take responsibility for breaking their part of the contract by failing to deliver. I have been pushed from pillar to post and today they say, the wont redeliver. Their customer service staff are so rude I have had to hang up as I couldnt talk to the man on the phone anymore. My anxiety is through the roof trying to get Zara to acknowledge me but they have my money and they dont care if I have my order or not. I most certainly wont be wasting my hard earned money at Zara anymore. I rather go to a small business who value customers and have some ethics. Zara has none whatsoever. Absolutely pathetic way to treat a loyal customer.

review_title: Ok experience in oxford st store!
review_text: Ok experience in oxford st store!
Worst delivery service... be prepared for multiple reschedules

Zara - Terrible Customer Service. My parcel was due to be delivered on 2nd Oct but said delayed for 24 hours. I have called, sent whats app messages, sent complaint emails, tweeted and sent a DM on twitter. They wont refund/reorder and keep telling me to wait. I advise everyone to never order online. The customer service is absolutely terrible. Its now the 10th Oct and I am still waiting for an update.

As per other reviewers, I waited for my delivery to be delivered on a chosen date for it to be delayed for 24 hours. It's a real shame Zara has started to use the sub standard Hermes for their deliveries as the clothes are always so good. Unfortunately Iwill not order from Zara after this experience.

I LOVED Zara for YEARS. Until my most recent experience. I purchased a dress shirt from their online store and it was delivered promptly. I wore the shirt ONE time, for a period of not even a few hours. And when I took the shirt off the entire seam of the sleeve had become unraveled all the way up to the elbow. I took pictures and filed a formal complaint. They called me a few weeks later and asked if I had washed the shirt. I had not washed the shirt at all and I told them I had not because I thought they might want to see it when I returned it. Then I waited ANOTHER three weeks to get a phone call to tell me that they didnt approve my complaint for replacement. Why would they even ask if it was washed if they were going to deny it anyways? Terrible quality, if Im going to spend $70+ on a dress shirt it should NOT become unraveled by the seam like that even after TEN uses, let alone ONE. Terrible customer service. Its kind of odd that they have ZERO customer reviews available on their website, isnt it? As someone who has purchased from them for more than the past 5 years I can promise you that I will not be purchasing from this entity again.

Horrible communication from an employee at Zara, Brent Cross Shopping Cnt this morning. I walked in with my partner to purchase a couple of items. I had a small espresso in my hand after about 5mins of being in to store this tall (male) staff member literally walked up to me and pointed at me and said not drinks - out. And walked off. Im a 45 year old man and did not appreciate being spoken to in that manner. I can appreciate that maybe you may have a no drinks policy but there is way to communicate with customers - its all in the delivery. Horrible guest experience.

review_title: No quality nor service friendly stores
review_text: I had a very bad experience in one of the Polish stores, I have raised a complaint, in 2 different sites, sent email with reminder and still no response. I bought a pair of trousers, twice faulty item and when I tried to change the second time I faced the an aggressive behavior from 2 of the store employees. I would not recommend the store for neither quality or service.

review_title: Zara has NIGHTMARE of customer service
review_text: Zara has NIGHTMARE of customer service. I agree with one post that I read, they have the cutest childrens clothing, but the minute something goes wrong, you are on your own. I bought a jacket for my child at a size which is already older than her age and its still too small. I had her try it in the store and she needed at least 3 sizes up than the suggested age. She is a regular sized girl. The jacket was in tags and original purchase condition. Unfortunately I lost the receipt and I asked the managers to help me exchange to another size, they coldly refused me. They said it was my own fault and they dont have to do anything about it. I had my daughter with me and I asked her to try the jacket on. It was indeed too small for a 5 year old when it clearly marked as age 6. I would have to get a size 8 for her to be perfect. The managers at Markville were so rude and rigid. They had absolutely no compassion. They didnt care if the child was there and the jacket was obviously too small due to their misleading size suggestion. These people have absolutely no manners or empathy. They are unwilling to listen or to help. The Managers acted all mighty and ruthless, but come on, you work at a clothing store, not a judge in a court house. What losers!

review_title: AVOID ZARA AT ALL COSTS! WHAT A JOKE
review_text: Absolutely disgusting customer service and non existent staff in the actual shop! Joke of a return service, emails ignored, so I had to drive to the nearest store over 20 miles away. To then be greeted by no staff, I stood at the counter for 10 minutes, knocking on the desk, shouting hello is anybody here?. I could have walked out with the every single item! Then a random worker saw me, carried on chewing her gum and pretended to be stroking a jumper?! Then what looked like an 11 year old girl appeared. She didnt have a clue how I could could exchange my dress for a different size so I got a refund. How this company is still in business I do not know!!!

review_title: Zara is a tricky and stole MY MONEY and MY BAG
review_text: I bought a bag from zara which leaked over all my clothes and damaged my clothes. This was a cheap quality bag. I then returned the bag to the store and zara then mentioned that will not help and not give me a refund due to the fact that they had printed an incorrect receipt for me on that day. This was clearly their fault and not mine. The receipt they printed said it was shoes when I had never bought shoes from them. And it was actually the bag. Eventually they got the bag back and I have never got my money back. They are very tricky with the way they label their products on the receipts. So please be careful. Their bag was very cheap quality. I feel cheated by them after what they have done as I was always a very loyal customer. Is it not the issue about the bag, it was also the way this whole case was dealt with. They basically stole my money, cheated me, printed a receipt with incorrect information and took the product back. I am extremely disappointed and hurt. Please do not trust this company. I used to shop from them a lot and I trusted them. Now I do not trust them at all after this incident and I am going to create awareness to other customers about this incident. If anyone wants to see pics of the bag or any other information please contact me and I am more than happy let you see what Zara really is. They were not even helpful to try and resolve the matter and the issue had been going on for more then 3 months. The bag at the end of the day was worth 17. I have spent literally over 2000+ on their products over the years. And they have now lost a valued customer. Until this is resolved, I will be creating awareness online and where ever I can to what they did to me. I will also not be buying anything else from them as they stole my money and stole the bag back ( as I was the owner of the bag, since I paid for it). I will also be looking at ways to take this further so if anyone would like to join me if they had bad experiences, please get in touch

Dreadful - as usual. Purchased a coat in London, security tag was left attached so off to Milton Keynes store to have it removed. 15 minute queue, loads of staff ambling around . happily ignoring us and then looking offended when someone asked if another till could be opened. This is Zara don't you know - we're worth waiting for! Eventually got to the counter and explained the situation, assistant snaps 'have you got the receipt?' then 'you bought this ages ago'. Surprisingly I have got better things to do than go to Zara to get THEIR mistakes rectified. So she went to the manager for approval as she has no autonomy and came back and repeated that I'd made the purchase months ago - I know when I purchased it - I was there and don't experience memory loss at this stage in my life but thanks for the reminder. I actually thought I might get an apology for the inconvenience caused but hey how could i be so stupid! This is Zara where the store assistant is king and the customer an irritation! I have now learnt this - thanks Zara staff for educating me - and will now lower my expectations accordingly. Nice coat though.

Enough with these big brands names yet they use humans as slaves. Zara cy is enslaving employees who are Europeans by not giving them contract letters when working, working two weeks straight without break, under paying them 880 euros a month instead of 1200 euros, working 46 hours or more then the legal hours of 38 set up by the labour laws, working on Sundays and not paying for over time. No sick leave. Several attempts at the labour office to look into the matter has been unsuccessful because they have them in their pockets: Louiza Valianti the labour relations inspector has never found anything wrong with Zara cy. Before she goes there she informs them then they prepare or present the workers they know will speak in their favour to her to speak to. When one speaks they say you can change job if you dont like it. Is time to speak up, it can be your mother, sister, wife, girlfriend or cousin working in these sweat shops.Employees dont stay long because of these mistreatment so they have less and those who are working are made to over work Zara Cyprus. Many are suffering for few to get rich. Help boycott their sales for them to know no one is above the law, it is people(costumers) making them rich not their smartness.

review_title: Not even one star is deserved
review_text: Not even one star is deserved. They have the worst customer service. My daughter bought a pair of jeans brought them hope took tags of put them on they ripped at belt line . Tried to return with tags receipt in hand . And because the mattress tag that they put strategically was removed they would not return or even give a new pair. That tag is purposely put in front so you will remove it because who wants a mattress tag sticking in your stomach. Such bad service opening up for class action .

What an absolute joke! Shocked and angered to receive a text and e-mail: We are sorry we missed you while delivering your ZARA order xxx. We will attempt it on 22/10. As I have obviously been home waiting for my order to be delivered. This courier did not even attempt to deliver my order! No buzz on the buzzer, no knock on the door! I ring ZARA customer service to be told nothing is guaranteed and also told it takes up to 24 hours for Paack to reply to complaints etc. By this time I should hopefully receive my order - but I must remember nothing is guaranteed! Want ZARA items go to the store because delivery sadly cant be guaranteed!
Disappointing - paid extra for next day delivery but received the parcel a week later. A shame considering they have such nice clothes at good prices. Wont be ordering online again.
Terrible customer service, don't ever order online from there

review_title: Anti-consumer policies and absent customer service
review_text: Possibly the worst exchange policy and customer service I have ever encountered. I bought 2 pairs of trousers and a jumper. When I got home I realised that I had lost the receipt, but wanted to return one pair of trousers, never been worn, labels intact and attached. When I returned to store, fully aware I couldnt get a refund (I didnt want a refund!) and wanted an exchange I was informed that the common, established customer goodwill policy of exchanging perfect goods within 28 days of purchase doesnt apply to Zara customers. Despite being able to provide: Bank statement as proof of purchase, the date, specific time, address of store...I was told that was not their policy and I can read their policy on the receipt. I had lost the receipt so how would I know? The vacuous sale staff do not tell you this at the point of purchase. There is no sign at the point of sale letting you know this policy (is this a lazy or illiteracy issue? Who knows. Other brands seem to manage it pretty easily). I then called customer services after being told by the staff at the store that they could provide me with a copy of my receipt. Clever move by the ignorant staff member in the shop to get me to to leave. The member customer service couldnt tell me why the policy is what it is. She genuinely didnt know, which shows how much Zara care about the training of their own staff: just putting them in the firing line for complaints with no information. Apparently Im due a phone call from someone who can explain it. I wont hold my breath. The death of the high street cant come quick enough. Zara is a perfect example as to why its dying.

review_title: Terrible customer service. Wont refund you!
review_text: Terrible customer service. I sent some items back but was only partially refunded what was in the box! They asked me for the proof of postage which had the weight of the parcel on it. I sent this to them but they still refused to refund me as they said their warehouse didnt received the item. It should be their responsibility to take it up with the courier as I have no evidence if the box was tampered with or not!!

Which the assistant was slowly putting every item on a hanger! Appalling knowing theres a customer waiting! I left, got better things to do than put up with this kind service. review_title: Zara in Jersey City, NJ Newport Mall
review_text: I went to Zara at Newport Mall in Jersey City, NJ to make a return. After waiting in their return line for 45+ minutes and returning my item, I then proceeded to shop. I purchased two items with a human cashier and left. Apparently the manager from Zara (short Hispanic male) told security I stole my items. Unbeknownst to me security is following me through the mall. Then finally they decided to approach me, four security guards, in front of everyone at the mall, who explained to me that that manager told them I stole my items. I showed them my receipt. I was extremely humiliated and embarrassed. Did he assume I was a thief because I had on a hoodie and was profiled? NEVER EVER EVER will I EVER shop there again. And Zara was one of my favorite stores.

review_title: errible Experience - Unreceived Order and No Refund

This issue occurred three months ago, and I have now made the decision to contact my lawyer to pursue a refund.

Royal mail delivered an empty, broken box after the delivery was delayed. Have sent zara several times with the proof however they are refusing to refund.

ZERO STARS for you ZARA! I have not received my package and I reached out the moment I received the 'order delivered' text message. I spoke to the customer service agents which did not help with anything but send me a 'denial of delivery' form. I then got an email with coordinates of some place that is NOT MY ADDRESS where my order was 'apparently delivered'. I am very disappointed and feel robbed by ZARA as this is not my first online order with them and have used the same address ever since- so can someone from ZARA explain where my order is???? This is a violation of my consumer rights. I have paid for the order + delivery fee and I expect to RECEIVE the order!

review_title: Do not trust Zara Trafford Centre to email your receipt
review_text: I visited the Trafford Centre store in early December. I bought 2 pairs of trousers. At the till I was advised to go to the self service till and input my email address to receive a receipt. I did so as the queues were very long. I have had the same email for 20 years so I input it correctly. To this day the receipt never arrived. As one pair didn't fit I needed to return this pair. I searched for the store nearest to where I live and it was Manchester, about an 80 mile round trip. So I decided to try online chat. This was absolutely hopeless and got me nowhere, just wasted an hour of my time. I would not advise anybody to trust this company for an email receipt. They are useless and customer service is diabolical. I would give zero stars if I could as I have lost almost ¬£40.

review_title: Ohhh I just came here to say howĶ
review_text: Ohhh I just came here to say how disappointed and awful the customer service is , but after reading the reviews , I realized I should have pay more attention to what people say about them online . Please STOP shopping at ZARA !!! They dont deserve our money

Absolutely despise Zaras service which is such a shame as their clothes are greatĶ they ride on the coattails of their success with being a popular high street brand but have completely lost their ability to provide good customer service. Staff look disinterested, the system to request different sizes of items in store and collect at the collection point is massively flawed - wait times are over 10-15min and sometimes you are told they cant find the item in their stockroom!! The queues for returns are the worst for waiting times and processing is sooo slow! Surely after all these poor reviews Zara should be waking up and making changes!

The company are currently transitioning to new technology such as self checkout to make the shopping experience quicker. Ive arrived to Westfield Stratford, London (busiest place to shop on the weekend). Only three sales assistants are available at the checkout with 20 unused self-checkout counters. Please explain how this acceptable, Ive waited for 30 minutes to return one item and this also the same queue for people who have newly shopped. There is no one to guide people on how to self-check out. The company keeps getting worse and worse falsely promoting better shopping experience. Please stop make us pay premium price and have horrible shopping experience for average quality clothes. I would rather pay 2x price and be treated well.

review_title: I recently placed an order with Zara

Each time I reach out , only to be told they will "look into it" and never hear back. It's been weeks, and there's still no resolution in sight. This level of service is completely unacceptable. I expected much better from a brand like Zara. I won't be shopping here again.

Zara online bunch of thieves. Placed an order for over ¬£100. Why has Zara not delivered the goods I ordered because they are thieves

I lost a full week due to this and missed the deadline by one day and they wouldn't offer me a gift card or anything. Terrible customer service and will not be shopping with them again anytime soon.

Horrible customer service the stuff came in 2 weeks and didnt fit me.
